races.name: Farmhouse Foods Novice Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Likealightswitch
runners.spotlight: Had plenty of chances before finally coming good in a maiden at Gowran (heavy); that was a weak race for which she went off at odds of 2-9; front-runner who can be keen and making all in this field won't be easy.
runners.comment: Maiden winner latest; front-runner can be keen and making all in this field won't be easy

runners.name: Combs
runners.spotlight: 1m4f winner; 0-6 as a hurdler but was third off this mark at Christmas and then ran into the smart Proactif here in January; the concern is his ordinary run last time at Down Royal (maiden).
runners.comment: Placed off this mark before; the concern is his ordinary run last time at Down Royal

runners.name: Arslan
runners.spotlight: It wasn't a strong maiden he won at Thurles (soft/heavy; beat two outsiders) but he did that very easily under positive tactics and he's clearly unexposed ahead of this first handicap; his yard has won the last two renewals.
runners.comment: Won his maiden easily and he's clearly unexposed ahead of this first handicap

runners.name: Desert Haven
runners.spotlight: Two respectable Flat runs last month but 0-10 over hurdles and has come up shy in less competitive races than this.
runners.comment: 0-10 over hurdles and come up shy in less competitive races than this

runners.name: Saveforarainyday
runners.spotlight: Fourth of 21 behind the smart Ballyfad in a Leopardstown maiden before winning very easily in a first-time hood (removed) at Limerick (heavy); this well-bred mare is potentially well handicapped despite top weight.
runners.comment: Hosed up in the hood at Limerick and this well-bred mare is potentially well handicapped

runners.name: Wackestone
runners.spotlight: Rated 80 on the Flat after three runs; still a maiden after four runs over hurdles but was placed behind respected opposition in the middle two and he has to be interest against older opposition on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Maiden promise; has to be interest against older opposition on handicap debut

runners.name: Kabylia
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; runner-up in three of her four maiden hurdles and she's likeably consistent; whether she's that well handicapped is another matter; hood added.
runners.comment: Likeably consistent but whether she's that well handicapped is another matter

runners.name: Radiator Springs
runners.spotlight: Winning pointer; nice run behind the classy Oldschool Outlaw before keeping on well enough to land a maiden at Clonmel (heavy); dangerously unexposed handicap debutant from a leading yard.
runners.comment: Third to a good horse before winning a maiden; dangerously unexposed handicap debutant

runners.name: Withabitofluk
runners.spotlight: Won Bellewstown bumper (good to yielding) last April; there's been sufficient promise in his maidens to believe he might be competitive off this sort of mark, though he has been absent for over a year and looks the yard second string on jockey bookings.
runners.comment: Unexposed handicap debutant; check the betting after more than a year off the track

runners.name: Hesgonethatway
runners.spotlight: Perhaps won too well at Naas (soft/heavy) as the 9lb rise was surely a factor in his defeat at Thurles, though he still ran okay in fairness; others are more intriguing here.
runners.comment: Perhaps won too well at Naas (soft/heavy) as the 9lb rise found him out at Thurles

runners.name: Walk On Annilog
runners.spotlight: Hasn't performed badly in either of her handicaps without looking ahead of her mark; this is no easier.
runners.comment: Hasn't performed badly in either of her handicaps without looking ahead of her mark

runners.name: Court Canyon
runners.spotlight: Point winner; won a maiden at Clonmel and ran well under a penalty at Catterick; has since left Gavin Cromwell; this mark looks no gift for handicap debut; tries a tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Point/maiden winner; left Gavin Cromwell; this mark looks no gift for handicap debut

runners.name: Mart Lane
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced 5yo who raised his game to finish second in a Wexford maiden (soft) and he brings potential off this sort of mark; respected.
runners.comment: Improved second in a maiden latest and he brings potential off this sort of mark

runners.name: Jet To Monte Carlo
runners.spotlight: Won a point 13 months ago; no closer than 12l in four runs under rules but this gelding will probably be seen in a brighter light sooner or later; market needs checking on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Will probably be seen in a brighter light sooner or later; check market on h'cap debut

runners.name: Kentucky Beach
runners.spotlight: 0-7; disappointing favourite at Tramore last time but has had a break since then and his previous seconds in 2m handicaps (in a tongue-tie) need respecting; each-way claims with tongue-tie back on.
runners.comment: Poor last time but had a break since; previous seconds make him of interest

runners.name: Lincoln Du Seuil
runners.spotlight: French bumper winner; beaten between 12l and 23l in his maiden hurdles; he's not obviously well in for this first handicap but it's early days and the potential is there to improve; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: He's not obviously well in for this first handicap but it's early days; new tongue-tie

runners.name: Tatateo
runners.spotlight: Well held in his two previous handicaps as a jumper but has since won a Cork novice and a staying handicap on the AW; others are a bit more intriguing, however; cheekpieces removed.
runners.comment: Well held in his two previous handicaps as a jumper but has since won a Cork novice

runners.name: Migdam
runners.spotlight: Only had two runs over hurdles, last time winning a maiden on good ground at Tramore (first-time hood); good and bad on the Flat since then; lacks jumping experience for a race of this nature.
runners.comment: Good and bad on the Flat since winning a maiden hurdle at Thurles; others preferred

runners.name: Thankyouforthedays
runners.spotlight: Quiet in his maidens but was loud in the market ahead of his handicap debut at Naas (soft to heavy) and duly won with any amount in hand; whacked up 13lb but he's well related and no doubt has a lot more to offer.
runners.comment: Whacked up 13lb for his Naas win but he's well related and no doubt has lots more to offer

runners.name: Poets Gale
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far in a Galway bumper on debut but hasn't kicked from that; wouldn't be the obvious answer on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Hasn't progressed from debut run and wouldn't be the obvious answer on handicap debut

runners.name: Camlin Park
runners.spotlight: Only 1-6 in points and her 12l maiden hurdle win at Downpatrick (2m2f, good) is very much a standout piece of form over hurdles; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Maiden win at Downpatrick very much a standout piece of form over hurdles

races.name: O'Driscoll's Irish Whiskey Juvenile Hurdle (Grade 2)

runners.name: Proactif
runners.spotlight: Reportedly never travelled when a disappointing favourite in the Triumph; he did beat the Cheltenham winner Apolon De Charnie in France and he looked good when accounting for Macho Man here on his stable debut; if forgiven his Festival blip, then his credentials are clear to see.
runners.comment: If forgiven his Festival blip in the Triumph then his credentials are clear to see

runners.name: Majolique
runners.spotlight: Beat respected opposition in a 1m2f French race; reportedly surprised her connections in winning so well at Naas (1m7f, heavy) and expected to come on leaps and bounds for that; engaged in a Grade 1 at Aintree next week but runs here; Townend's chosen one.
runners.comment: 1m2f French winner; impressed at Naas when widely expected to need the run

runners.name: North Shore
runners.spotlight: 1m4f winner in France; twice beaten about 11l behind the smart Narciso Has and, for whatever reason, he didn't appear to give his true running in the Triumph (16-1); could easily be given another chance back in a Grade 2.
runners.comment: Never gave his true running in the Triumph and can be given another chance in a Grade 2

runners.name: Moulin Labbe
runners.spotlight: French bumper winner; weak in the betting when a remote fifth on his hurdle/stable debut but then odds-on when coming home 13l to the good at Navan (2m, heavy); raised in grade here but considered a chaser in the making and clearly open to vast improvement.
runners.comment: 13l winner at Navan; chaser in the making and clearly open to vast improvement

runners.name: Macho Man
runners.spotlight: Easy winner of his only hurdle run in France; inexperience evident when chasing home Proactif here in January (yielding; finished his race off well); only 12-1 for the Triumph and yet all hopes went west with a bad mid-race blunder after which he was pulled up; remains very promising.
runners.comment: 12-1 for the Triumph and yet his race ended after a bad mid-race blunder; promising

runners.name: Harwa
runners.spotlight: Twice runner-up in France; well on top late on to win his maiden here (heavy) and it was no great surprise to see him struggle in the Fred Winter off what looked a tough opening mark (good to soft; 28-1); others have superior form.
runners.comment: Course maiden winner; looked on a tough mark in the Fred Winter and ran accordingly

runners.name: Adrienne
runners.spotlight: No great shakes in failing to win on the Flat but has won a maiden over hurdles and performed well in the face of some stiff tasks; however, this is another, and she's held by Majolique on Naas form.
runners.comment: Maiden winner but has been getting found out in races of this nature

runners.name: Kai Lung
runners.spotlight: Consistent Flat horse in France; had the Fred Winter winner Saratoga back in third when winning his maiden at Naas (first-time hood; heavy) and unwise to read too much into his no-show in the Triumph (33-1) as he lost his position after mid-race interference; remains capable of better.
runners.comment: Maiden win reads well; suffered interference when pulled up in the Triumph

runners.name: Michonne
runners.spotlight: Bought for 185,000euros out of top French Flat yard for whom she won at 1m3f and 2m; well held in a Leopardstown maiden over Christmas but much better when second to Majolique at Naas and looks capable of holding her own at this level.
runners.comment: Second to Majolique at Naas and looks capable of holding her own at this level

races.name: Fairyhouse Steel Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Timeless Treaty
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner over hurdles, a maiden and two novices, the most recent at Thurles in February (20.5f, soft); last two runs have come at that venue, a close third in a Grade 3 and a good second behind a 146-rated chaser in a conditions hurdle; has not fared well in previous handicap runs but respected.
runners.comment: Three-time winner, has not fared well in previous handicap runs but respected

runners.name: Buachaillbocht
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner won a novice chase at this meeting last year; third on seasonal return in a Navan handicap hurdle but last two efforts over fences have not been encouraging; back over timber now and new headgear combination needs to revive.
runners.comment: Won novice chase at this meeting last year, poor over fences last twice, new headgear now

runners.name: El Champo
runners.spotlight: Three wins over timber, the most recent at Galway in October 2023; two chase wins in 2025 but pulled up in that sphere on latest at Gowran a month ago; 4lb wrong back over hurdles; others preferred.
runners.comment: Last hurdles win in 2023, two chase wins in 2025, pulled up latest, 4lb wrong here

runners.name: Fierce Handay
runners.spotlight: Sole success came in 2m handicap here in December on soft; followed that up with a solid second over 20.5f (gd-yld) at Leopardstown and just 2lb higher here; struggled at 2m on testing ground the last twice, but interesting now back up in trip at the scene of his only win with Cobden taking over.
runners.comment: 2m course winner, fine 2m4f run at Leop', testing ground excuses last twice, Cobden up

runners.name: Fad Eadrainn
runners.spotlight: Beat some useful sorts when scoring in a rated hurdle here in November (20.4f, sft-hvy) but struggled in handicaps on next two starts and his third here on latest in February was a remote one; just 1lb lower now; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Good win in rated hurdle here in Nov' but out of sorts since, recent third a remote one

runners.name: Kovanis
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point, bumper and maiden hurdle winner; highly tried to no avail the last twice in a Grade 2 novice at Navan and in a Grade 1 novice at Naas (both 2m4f on soft and heavy); makes handicap debut now and preferred by Kennedy, but others appeal more.
runners.comment: Point, bumper and maiden winner, outclassed last twice, h'cap debut, others preferred

runners.name: Jony R
runners.spotlight: Both wins at Down Royal, a 2m1f maiden (yielding) and a 2m1f novice (soft); came up short when tried in a handicap at Leopardstown in December and also in Listed and Grade 3 races on testing ground the last twice; stamina to prove back in a handicap, but return to better ground may help.
runners.comment: Two wins at Down Royal, came up short in listed and Gr' 3 last twice, stamina to prove

runners.name: Welonlyhavedone
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner won a Galway maiden (16.5f, yielding) on his hurdling debut in October; fell at the last when holding every chance in a 20.5f novice back there later that month; has pulled up in two of his last three starts and now makes handicap debut; risky.
runners.comment: Galway maiden winner, pulled up in two of last three starts, risky on h'cap debut

runners.name: Blue Mosque
runners.spotlight: Cork maiden hurdle winner in November 2024 (2m4f, good); struggled in first two runs this season at Leopardstown but signs of life when a 13l fourth at Naas (2m3f, sft-hvy) 29 days ago; the return to better ground here will suit and yard had a double at the Curragh on Good Friday; could go well.
runners.comment: Signs of life when fourth at Naas latest, ground will suit, yard in form, could go well

runners.name: Shabby Hill
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner in March 2024; stepped forward from low-key rules debut at Punchestown when winning a maiden at that venue in January (19.5f, sft-hvy); added a Navan novice hurdle (22.5f, soft) 16 days ago; could go well on handicap bow if acting on better ground.
runners.comment: Point winner, maiden and novice wins last twice, h'cap debutant could go well

runners.name: Eclipse Chaser
runners.spotlight: With top yard but has failed to build on his September Navan maiden hurdle victory (2m5f, yielding); beaten 27l and then pulled up in first two handicaps at Leopardstown on contrasting ground; others preferred.
runners.comment: Struggled since Galway maiden win, no impact in h'caps the last twice, others appeal more

runners.name: Granns Boy
runners.spotlight: Won at this meeting last year after a break and has generally held form well since, adding wins over hurdles at Listowel and on the Flat at Thurles; went close at Galway in October on last run over timber and just 2lb higher here; pleasing return on the Flat at Navan last week; interesting.
runners.comment: Won on this card last year, fit from recent run on the Flat, interesting runner

runners.name: Eagles Reign
runners.spotlight: Two wins in 2024 but no joy since; back to form with a 3.25l second at Naas (2m3f, sft-hvy) 29 days ago but remains 6lb above his last win and he's 2lb higher than when beaten 6.5l in this last year; would probably prefer softer ground too.
runners.comment: 6.5l runner up in this last year, 2lb higher now and would probably prefer it softer

runners.name: Slane Hill
runners.spotlight: Two-time hurdles winner but on losing run since November 2024 success at Navan (2m4f, gd-yld); below best in handicap chase when last seen at Kilbeggan in August and Sean Flanagan seemingly prefers Hgranca De Thaix.
runners.comment: No win since Nov' 2024, below best when last seen in August, Flanagan prefers stablemate

runners.name: Hgranca De Thaix
runners.spotlight: Rated 9lb higher over fences but has so far failed to exploit his lower hurdles mark this season; went very close to doing so at Leopardstown (20.5f, gd-yld) in December and Navan (2m4f, sft-hvy) in January but below that level at Naas on latest; could rebound.
runners.comment: Rated 9lb higher over fences, below best at Naas latest but could rebound

runners.name: Must Go Now
runners.spotlight: Second runner for local yard; sole win came here over 2m4f (yld-sft) in January 2025; below par this season until producing a fine run to finish a close third at Cork (2m4f, yielding) in first-time cheekpieces (discarded); 3lb wrong here but rider's claim negates that; can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Course winner, back to form latest, 3lb wrong but rider claims 7lb, can't discount

runners.name: Meet And Greet
runners.spotlight: No win since December 2024 and recent chase efforts have not suggested a return to the winners' enclosure is imminent; new cheekpieces need to spark a revival back over hurdles.
runners.comment: No win since Dec' 2024, recent chase runs poor, cheekpieces need to spark revival

races.name: Rathbarry & Glenview Studs Hurdle (Grade 2)

runners.name: Bowensonfire
runners.spotlight: Admirably consistent handicapper who didn't run too badly when midfield in the County Hurdle last month; however, this is a step up the ladder for him and regular rider Jack Kennedy evidently prefers the chances of The Yellow Clay.
runners.comment: Admirably consistent handicapper; Jack Kennedy prefers the chances of The Yellow Clay

runners.name: Storm Heart
runners.spotlight: Triumph favourite two years ago (finished fifth); very lightly raced in the meantime and firmly on an upward curve in winning twice on heavy ground (including a Grade 3) before an admirable third in the 2m5f handicap at Cheltenham (good to soft) where he would have been closer but for hitting two out; top connections haven't got to the bottom of him yet; clear claims.
runners.comment: Better than ever this season and unlucky not to be closer at Cheltenham (2m5f handicap)

runners.name: Slade Steel
runners.spotlight: Supreme winner two seasons ago; 0-7 in the meantime and has been chasing, last time beaten the thick end of 20l when one of the favourites for the 2m4f handicap at Cheltenham; if this return to hurdling and the first-time cheekpieces float his boat, then he might not be far away.
runners.comment: 0-7 since winning the 2024 Supreme and has been chasing; cheekpieces given a go

runners.name: The Yellow Clay
runners.spotlight: High class novice last season who was second to The New Lion in the 2m5f Grade 1 at Cheltenham; later suffered a heavy fall at Punchestown; this campaign hasn't really got off the ground and departed too far out (three from home) in the 2m5f handicap at the Festival (Storm Heart third) to know how he would have fared, though was going well enough at the time; wouldn't be a surprise to see him get his career back on track.
runners.comment: Classy novice; going okay when departing three out in a handicap at Cheltenham

runners.name: Farren Glory
runners.spotlight: Grade 1-winning novice and won over fences last season; hasn't really reached the heights he once promised and was beaten in races won by Glen Kiln and Storm Heart before pulling up in the 2m5f handicap at Cheltenham when tried in cheekpieces (retained).
runners.comment: Held by some of these and last time he was pulled up in a handicap at Cheltenham

runners.name: Kawaboomga
runners.spotlight: Smart novice who wasn't beaten far by Kopek Des Bordes in a maiden before beating the smart William Munny next time out (on soft); finished behind Storm Heart and Farren Glory in the Grade 3 Red Mills at Gowran (heavy) but ran well after a long absence and he should be considerably sharper here; 3lb better off with Storm Heart.
runners.comment: Eyecatching novice form; should be all the sharper for his belated return at Gowran

runners.name: Brewinupastorm
runners.spotlight: Won this race two years ago and returns on the back of finishing second in the Grade 2 National Spirit at Fontwell, a race he'd won twice before; however, he's well into the veteran stage and looks vulnerable.
runners.comment: Previous winner of this race but well into the veteran stage and looks vulnerable

runners.name: Blizzard Of Oz
runners.spotlight: Got his career back on track on returning to hurdles at Thurles (2m7f, soft to heavy), but that was still beneath his peak form and Paul Townend deserts him for Storm Heart.
runners.comment: Still below his best when winning at Thurles; Paul Townend deserts him for Storm Heart

runners.name: Grainne A Chroi
runners.spotlight: Doubled her tally under rules when emerging from off the pace to narrowly win the Grade 3 Quevega at Punchestown (2m4f, soft); this is appreciably more demanding against male opposition and has her work cut out at the weights.
runners.comment: This is more demanding against male opposition and has her work cut out at the weights
runners.owner: Ms Eileen OBrien & John Anthony Harrington

runners.name: Glen Kiln
runners.spotlight: Better than ever this season and has contested two Grade 1s; appreciated the drop in class when making all in a Grade 3 at Naas (2m, heavy) and this 7yo is a good bet to give his running (stays this far); very sound jumper.
runners.comment: Appreciated the drop in grade when making all in a Grade 3 at Naas; commands respect

races.name: Underwriting Exchange Fairyhouse Chase (Grade 2)

runners.name: Found A Fifty
runners.spotlight: Eight-time chase winner; beat Majborough and Energumene at Cork in December; won this race last year, holding off the late challenge of Saint Sam (Blood Destiny a 7.25l third); pulled up in the Champion Chase last time, but this is more his level and he is the one to beat.
runners.comment: Beat Energumene at Cork in Dec', won this last year, pulled up latest but the one to beat

runners.name: Down Memory Lane
runners.spotlight: Won his bumper here; both chase wins have come at Navan; won a 20.5f Listed handicap chase on testing ground there off 144 in December; disappointed when third-favourite for the Plate Handicap at Cheltenham; better than that and has a good chance at the weights; can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Both chase wins at Navan, disappointed at Cheltenham latest, could bounce back

runners.name: Saint Sam
runners.spotlight: Six-time chase winner but no success since a Grade 2 win at Clonmel in November 2024; ran a cracker in this last year to finish a 0.75l second behind Found A Fifty; below par in both runs this season and needs to leave those tame efforts behind.
runners.comment: Runner up in this last year behind Found A Fifty, needs to leave last two efforts behind

runners.name: Sa Fureur
runners.spotlight: 2-6 over fences; won the Navan Listed handicap in 2024, that Down Memory Lane won last year, off 143, which was his last success; excellent effort in the Grade 2 Red Mills at Gowran in February when a 1.25l second behind Impaire Et Passe; well beaten in the Martin Pipe latest but the return to chasing is a plus; a fair bit to find on the figures, however.
runners.comment: Shaped nicely behind Impaire Et Passe on penultimate, a bit to find on the figures here

runners.name: Blood Destiny
runners.spotlight: Four-time chase winner, including two Grade 3s; pulled up in a Grade 3 here on penultimate start but bounced back straight away with a Grade 3 win at Naas (2m, heavy); came up short in this last year when a 7.25l third and needs to find more to feature at the finish.
runners.comment: Grade 3 win at Naas latest, came up short in this last year, needs to find more to feature

runners.name: Energumene
runners.spotlight: High class operator in his pomp; has won eleven chases, including six at the top table; however, it seems like time is catching up with the 12yo and he was well held by Found A Fifty at Cork in December; tailed off at Leopardstown on latest in first-time cheekpieces (discarded); last ran at this trip in 2020; others preferred.
runners.comment: Top class in pomp but time catching up with him, held by Found A Fifty on Cork run in Dec'

races.name: BOYLE Sports Irish Grand National Chase (Extended Handicap Chase) (Grade 3)

runners.name: The Enabler
runners.spotlight: Impressed with a 15l win over 2m5f at this venue in December on second chase start; two lesser runs followed in Grade 3 races; travelled well for a long way in the Kim Muir before weakening in the last half-furlong; that raises a doubt about his stamina.
runners.comment: Weakened in the last half-furlong of the Kim Muir, that raises a doubt about his stamina

runners.name: CEst Ta Chance
runners.spotlight: Achieved a peak rating of 135 over hurdles; midfield when beaten 20l on chase debut at this venue; little impression when beaten almost 30l in fourth at Leopardstown; much improved when beating two shorter-priced stablemates at Gowran; confirmed the form of that race with Soldier In Milan when landing a Grade 3 event at Thurles.
runners.comment: Confirmed Gowran form with Soldier In Milan when landing a Grade 3 event at Thurles

runners.name: Kiss Will
runners.spotlight: Hurdles form comprised 2m/3m wins (including Listed) either side of a comprehensive defeat in the Turners at the 2025 Cheltenham Festival; still a maiden after four runs over fences; decent placed form linked to Flicker Of Hope and Soldier In Milan; faded from the last when fifth (third best of yard's runners) in the Jack Richards at Cheltenham.
runners.comment: Still a maiden after four runs over fences, potential for improvement, Paul Townend rides

runners.name: Kurasso Blue
runners.spotlight: 2-3 over hurdles; saw off useful opposition at Punchestown (2m7f, heavy) in December in his second chase; runner-up to Flicker Of Hope at Naas (almost 3m1f, soft) in January; jumped badly and pulled up four out when 7-2 second favourite for the National Hunt Chase; cheekpieces on now.
runners.comment: Jumped badly and pulled up four out when 7-2 second favourite for the National Hunt Chase

runners.name: The Jukebox Kid
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner in 2024; won on the first of two starts over hurdles last season; made winning chase debut at Carlisle in November; suffered a reverse at Cheltenham before making amends with two 3m Ascot wins, in a handicap and the Grade 2 Reynoldstown; good jumper with considerable potential for improvement,
runners.comment: 3-4 over fences, a good jumper with considerable potential for improvement, strong chance

runners.name: One Big Bang
runners.spotlight: Fifth in last year's Pertemps Final and ended the season with a creditable third in a handicap hurdle at the Punchestown festival; this season's chase win came in a match at Southwell and he had run only in small-field races over fences until a lively display to take third place in the 3m6f National Hunt Chase on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Lively display when third in the 3m6f National Hunt Chase on handicap debut, good chance

runners.name: Better Times Ahead
runners.spotlight: In top form this season, won a handicap hurdle off 108 at this venue in November; followed up by beating Will Do narrowly in the Porterstown Chase here in December (3m6f, yielding to soft); left in the lead at the final fence in the Thyestes before being overhauled by Now Is The Hour (3m1f, heavy); probably found 2m3f inadequate over hurdles on latest.
runners.comment: Maintained progression when second in the Thyestes, kept ticking over with a hurdles run

runners.name: Better Days Ahead
runners.spotlight: Won two small-field chases (including a Grade 2) at Navan last season and third in the Brown Advisory; faded from three out when sixth behind Haiti Couleurs in this race last year; ran in two small-field races at Navan in February; rider's claim takes some of the sting from his hefty burden; one of six for the stable.
runners.comment: Sixth in this last year, rider's valuable 7lb claim eases burden, one of six for Elliott

runners.name: Showurappreciation
runners.spotlight: Shaped well on handicap chase debut at Navan when third to The Lovely Man (3m, soft to heavy); built on that when winning at Punchestown (2m7f, soft to heavy); took advantage of his lower hurdles mark on his next start; maintained progression in winning a valuable novice handicap at Navan; may remain competitive under a 9lb penalty.
runners.comment: Maintained progression in winning at Navan last month, definite chance despite 9lb penalty

runners.name: Waterford Whispers
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in the Martin Pipe Handicap Hurdle in 2024; 0-7 over fences but he returned to form when third in a big-field handicap at Leopardstown (extended 2m5f, soft) in February; failed to run to expectations in the Kim Muir (9-2 chance), but unwise to discount; trainer's sole runner.
runners.comment: Failed to run to expectations in the Kim Muir, unwise to discount, trainer's sole runner

runners.name: OToole
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced 10yo whose initial chase win came in a 2m4f Newcastle handicap in 2023; more recently won a 3m1f Ayr handicap hurdle early last year; seasonal return over fences at Cork in November was encouraging; bounced back from a poor Haydock run when winning a Listed handicap chase over an extended 2m5f at Leopardstown.
runners.comment: Won a Listed handicap chase over an extended 2m5f at Leopardstown last time, stays 3m-plus

runners.name: Shecouldbeanything
runners.spotlight: Four wins last season included 2m4f Grade 3 Tipperary novice; also finished third to Dinoblue at Cheltenham; reappeared with a fine third in the Galway Plate in July; pulled up in Kerry National, two veterinary excuses offered; managed a creditable third in the Thyestes despite a couple of sluggish jumps; Jack Kennedy aboard.
runners.comment: Creditable third in the Thyestes despite a couple of sluggish jumps, Jack Kennedy aboard

runners.name: Shanbally Kid
runners.spotlight: Came good over fences at last year's Punchestown festival in a 23-runner extended 3m7f handicap, winning by 15l; took a hefty 14lb rise for that; never dangerous on seasonal return at Leopardstown and pulled up in the Thyestes; needs to jump better than when fourth in the Leinster National.
runners.comment: 3m7f Punchestown winner, needs to jump better than when fourth in the Leinster National

runners.name: Search For Glory
runners.spotlight: Disputing the lead when unseating at the last in the Ultima at Cheltenham, a fine effort against the background of his failure (pulled up) in the Thyestes at Gowran; if none the worse for his Cheltenham experience, has a clear chance on his second placing in the Paddy Power Chase at Leopardstown (3m, yielding).
runners.comment: Good form before Thyestes flop, disputing lead when unseating at the last in the Ultima

runners.name: Goraibhmaithagat
runners.spotlight: Point and hurdles winner; ordinary form in opening chase runs; signs of promise in his third on handicap chase debut at Naas in January (2m4f, heavy); well backed in a Listed handicap at the same venue on his next start, landing the gamble despite some sloppy jumping; just run out of it in the 3m1f Leinster National last time; perhaps not sure to stay this far.
runners.comment: Just run out of it in the 3m1f Leinster National, perhaps not sure to stay this far

runners.name: Argento Boy
runners.spotlight: Made amends for a fall on his chasing debut with wins in a beginners' event at Punchestown in December and Naas Grade 3 in January (extended 3m, heavy); struggled in the Brown Advisory (won by stablemate Kitzbuhel) on ground that was probably too lively; one of seven for the stable.
runners.comment: Smart novice, ground too lively for him in the Brown Advisory, one of seven for Mullins

runners.name: Soldier In Milan
runners.spotlight: Point winner in 2024; bumper winner at Punchestown last April (2m3f, yielding); clinically abnormal when pulled up on chasing debut; not beaten far in fourth behind C'Est Ta Chance at Gowran (2m4f, heavy) in January; game winner from Kiss Will over 2m7f at Punchestown; fairly tame effort behind C'Est Ta Chance at Thurles; could bounce back to produce a big run.
runners.comment: Beat Kiss Will over 2m7f at Punchestown, has to reverse Thurles form with C'Est Ta Chance

runners.name: Western Walk
runners.spotlight: Very useful hurdler at up to 3m (rated 132); yet to win over fences, but not disgraced in Grade 1 behind Final Demand at Limerick in December; third behind two smart types in Navan Grade 2 at Navan; kept ticking over with a hurdles run last month.
runners.comment: Decent hurdler at up to 3m (rated 132), yet to win over fences, handicap debut off 135

runners.name: Rushmount
runners.spotlight: All three wins at Thurles; improved to win a handicap there (extended 2m5f, yielding) in December; form has worked out well; back in his comfort zone after an ambitious run in the Brown Advisory.
runners.comment: Back in his comfort zone after an ambitious Brown Advisory run, one of three for stable

runners.name: Yeah Man
runners.spotlight: Won the 2024 Grand National Trial at Haydock and placed in the last two editions of the Troytown at Navan; two most recent runs have been over hurdles; made no impact in the Pertemps Final at Cheltenham; 50-1 when unseated at the eighth in last year's race.
runners.comment: Last two runs have been over hurdles, has shown good stamina in valuable handicap chases

runners.name: Karia Des Blaises
runners.spotlight: French hurdle winner; useful form in defeat in four chase starts last season, was lying second when falling at the last in Limerick handicap chase on return at Christmas; got away with a 2lb rise for a very easy success in a Listed chase at Thurles.
runners.comment: Got away with a 2lb rise for an easy win in a Listed chase at Thurles, big jump in class

runners.name: Flicker Of Hope
runners.spotlight: Smart hurdler who has taken really well to chasing; won a 2m4f beginners' event at this venue (heavy) and relished step up to an extended 3m at Naas when outstaying odds-on favourite Kurasso Blue on soft in January; flopped in a Grade 3 won by Argento Boy at the same venue later that month.
runners.comment: Good winner at Naas in January, flopped in a race won by Argento Boy at  the same venue

runners.name: Weveallbeencaught
runners.spotlight: Best effort this season when second in the Munster National in October (3m, yielding to soft); has a bit to find with Monbeg Genius on running in the Kim Muir (finished seventh), but closely matched with The Enabler and Waterford Whispers on that showing.
runners.comment: Second in Munster National, a bit to find with Monbeg Genius on running in the Kim Muir

runners.name: Joystick
runners.spotlight: Big improvement on third chase start when beating Slade Steel at Navan (3m, soft to heavy) in January; out of his depth in the Brown Advisory; 15lb higher than his hurdles mark, not attractively handicapped; tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Not attractively handicapped, 15lb higher than hurdles mark, yard has better candidates

runners.name: Monbeg Genius
runners.spotlight: Third in the Ultima at Cheltenham (3m1f, soft) in March 2023; in good form this season; ran respectably in the Welsh National (3m6f, good to soft) and Haydock Grand National Trial (3m4f, good to soft); had all but one of the Irish challengers behind when a fine third in the Kim Muir.
runners.comment: Had all but one of the Irish raiders behind when a fine third in the Kim Muir, respected

runners.name: Born Braver
runners.spotlight: 2-4 over hurdles; modest form in beginners' chases; much improved when a neck second over 3m at Down Royal on second handicap start, underlining the stamina shown when winning two points and a 3m hurdle; failed to build on that in the Grand National Trial at Punchestown; appreciated better ground when landing the 3m4f Ulster National; has picked up an 8lb penalty.
runners.comment: Appreciated better ground when landing the 3m4f Ulster National, has an 8lb penalty

runners.name: Sa Majeste
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner last term and ran a fine race in the Kim Muir over 3m2f at Cheltenham; pulled up in this last year, and struggled this season until showing definite signs of a return to form when third in the 3m1f Leinster National at Naas last month.
runners.comment: Definite signs of a return to form when third in the 3m1f Leinster National at Naas

runners.name: Duffle Coat
runners.spotlight: 1-18 over fences; out of form since second in the 2024 Galway Plate; 50-1 when pulled up in last year's edition of this race; trainer has stronger candidates.
runners.comment: 1-18 over fences, out of form since second in the 2024 Galway Plate, hard to make a case

runners.name: Hartur dArc
runners.spotlight: Finished second in last year's Leinster National at Naas; completely out of form over hurdles on his last two starts, previously down the field in the Paddy Power at Leopardstown.
runners.comment: Down the field in the Paddy Power at Leopardstown, out of form over hurdles more recently

runners.name: Velvet Elvis
runners.spotlight: Peak form includes a clear second to subsequent Grand National winner Nick Rockett in Thyestes Chase at Gowran (3m1f, soft to heavy) for Gavin Cromwell last season; pulled up in the Troytown on stable debut and has continued to struggle; failed to take to the cross-country course at Cheltenham.
runners.comment: Pulled up in the Troytown on stable debut and has continued to struggle, faces huge task

races.name: Join Racing TV Now Handicap Chase

runners.name: Ayiko
runners.spotlight: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner, both at Ayr; best of three chase efforts came at Haydock in December when 2l behind the 124-rated Lario; has run well here over timber and he can't be fully dismissed in a new headgear combination.
runners.comment: Ayr Bumper and maiden winner, best chase run at Haydock, new headgear combo, not dismissed

runners.name: Killaney King
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner yet to get his head in front under rules; promise in both chase starts and weighted to close the 7.75l gap to Joyau De Thaix who won the beginners' chase he contested at Leopardstown last time.
runners.comment: Point winner, promise both chase starts, weighted to get closer to Joyau De Thaix

runners.name: Butch Cassidy
runners.spotlight: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner; best chase effort came over 2m7f on heavy in a Punchestown beginners' on penultimate when 3.75l behind the now 142-rated Kurasso Blue (received 8lb); not as good at Gowran latest (2m4f, heavy) but could be on a nice mark for handicap debut based on that Punchestown run.
runners.comment: Bumper and maiden winner, could be on a nice mark based on Punchestown third

runners.name: Patter Merchant
runners.spotlight: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner is 0-6 over fences; ran well in a beginners' here in November but beaten 21l and pulled up in first two handicap chases at Punchestown and Cheltenham; others have more appealing profiles.
runners.comment: 0-6 over fences, poor efforts in first two handicap chases, others appeal more

runners.name: Union Station
runners.spotlight: Maiden went close to landing breakthrough win at Leopardstown (24.5f, yielding) in December off 3lb lower; unseated over 2m in a beginners' here, then finished a modest fourth over 3m6f at Cheltenham; 6lb lower here but needs to find extra to shed the maiden tag.
runners.comment: Maiden went close off 3lb lower at Leopardstown in Dec', modest fourth latest, needs more

runners.name: Will Do
runners.spotlight: Bumper and two-time hurdle winner; 0-14 in chases but has run well in defeat at times, most notably over 3m6f at the Cheltenham Festival in 2025 and when a neck second in the Porterstown here last November (3m6f, yielding); out of sorts in three runs since, though, and needs to rediscover best.
runners.comment: 0-14 over fences, fine run in Porterstown here in Nov', poor thrice since, needs to revive

runners.name: Well Del
runners.spotlight: Won a handicap hurdle on first start for this yard at Punchestown in February; won a beginners' chase for Liam O'Brien in October 2024; has run well here before but well beaten last twice over timber and this looks too demanding; cheekpieces go on.
runners.comment: Won first start for this yard over timber, well beaten last twice, this too demanding

runners.name: Prends Garde A Toi
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent since opening chase account in a beginners' at Naas in December (2m4f, heavy); ran well when a 5.75l fourth of 14 in a valuable handicap at Punchestown in February but pulled up at Cheltenham on latest; capable of better and preferred by Jack Kennedy.
runners.comment: Uneven form since beginners' win at Naas, pulled up latest but capable of better

runners.name: Prophets Corner
runners.spotlight: Four-time point-to-point winner gained first chase win under rules' on latest at Limerick (24.5f, sft-hvy); raised 6lb for that and this is a deeper race; will need a big career-best to follow up.
runners.comment: Up 6lb for first chase win at Limerick latest, this a deeper race, big career best needed

runners.name: Savante
runners.spotlight: Touched off in a 3m Listed handicap hurdle at Leopardstown (heavy) in February off 121; made amends in a mares' handicap chase at Navan (20.5f, sft-hvy) later that month, winning readily by 9l; hiked up 10lb to 122 and steps back up in trip; could be more to come.
runners.comment: Up 10lb for mares' handicap chase win latest, step up in trip fine, may be more to come

runners.name: Joyau De Thaix
runners.spotlight: Two-time hurdle winner landed a first chase success in a Leopardstown beginners' last month (21.5f, yielding) with Killaney King over 7l back in third; has won at this trip over hurdles and his opening chase mark looks fair; ground fine too and could go well.
runners.comment: Off the mark over fences in Leop' beginners' latest, opening chase mark looks fair

runners.name: Barry Lyndon
runners.spotlight: One of his three hurdles wins came here, as did his only success over fences in a beginners' in October (2m5f, yielding); well beaten in a 2m5f novice here and a Grade 3 at Thurles and stamina to prove in first handicap chase.
runners.comment: Two-time course winner, below best in novice and Gr'3 last twice, stamina to prove

runners.name: Supreme Gift
runners.spotlight: Useful performer for Henry Daly, winning six races, including an Ascot handicap chase; modest form in two starts for this stable, albeit trip and testing ground probably not ideal on latest; new cheekpieces need to spark.
runners.comment: Useful for Henry Daly, below best first two runs for this yard, cheekpieces need to spark

runners.name: Cave Court
runners.spotlight: Two-time chase winner; fine effort when runner-up in a Cartmel handicap chase in June 2025 but only seen three times since and well beaten both starts in 2026, over hurdles at Haydock and in the Kim Muir at Cheltenham; needs to rediscover best.
runners.comment: Lost way since fine Cartmel run in June 2025, well beaten last twice, needs to find best

runners.name: Ney
runners.spotlight: 2-6 over fences; won back to back races at Punchestown (22.5f, soft) and a novice here (3m2f, soft) last year; modest enough form this season but did run a nice race when a 5.5l second of 13 on penultimate at Punchestown (22.5f, sft-hvy); not as good back there on latest but could rebound.
runners.comment: Dual chase winner inc' here, sound effort on penultimate, not as good latest, may rebound

runners.name: Hermino AA
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner for Gary Moore; yet to get his head in front in six chase runs; soundly beaten both completed starts for this stable and pulled up on latest at Navan (3m, heavy); leap of faith required to back him here.
runners.comment: Won three hurdles for Gary Moore, poor in three runs for this yard, leap of faith required

runners.name: Clonmeen
runners.spotlight: Promise here on seasonal reappearance; failed to build on that on next four starts but signs of a revival when staying on for a 9.5l fourth back here (3m, heavy) on latest; blinkers could eke out more but he's 7lb wrong and looks up against it.
runners.comment: Best runs this season have come at this venue, 7lb wrong here, looks up against it

runners.name: Chosen Witness
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over fences and hasn't won since scoring over hurdles at Ayr in April 2024; failed to complete in two of three starts this season and beaten 63l at Leopardstown when he did get around; top yard and mark tumbling but hard to make a case for on form.
runners.comment: On long losing run, failed to complete two of last three starts, hard to make a case for

races.name: Leinster Reinforcements & Brazil Piling (Pro/Am) Flat Race

runners.name: Poetisa
runners.spotlight: Changed hands for Â£300,000 after winning a Cheltenham bumper last April for Toby Bulgin; runner-up in two hurdles (one a Listed) and a bumper for Willie Mullins, performing to a useful level; gets weight off male opposition this time and she may appreciate not having to contend with heavy ground (weather permitting).
runners.comment: Cheltenham bumper winner; three seconds for this yard but performed to a good level

runners.name: Charismatic Kid
runners.spotlight: Bought for Â£300,000 soon after winning a Navan bumper in early December; he took a tall reputation into a Grade 2 at Leopardstown in early February and was backed accordingly, only for his effort to peter out after briefly threatening up the straight; the runner-up wasn't beaten that far at Cheltenham and this 5yo surely has a lot more to offer.
runners.comment: Well backed when coming up short in a Grade at the DRF; open to improvement

runners.name: State Pension
runners.spotlight: Beat four rivals on good to yielding at Tipperary and the ground was heavy when third at Naas; less taxing conditions here may help, but others are higher up the pecking order.
runners.comment: Good/yielding winner; the ground was heavy when only third at Naas

runners.name: Jeroboam Machin
runners.spotlight: Point winner who looked a top prospect when beating smart opposition in the Grade 2 bumper at the 2024 Dublin Racing Festival; penalised accordingly but perhaps a greater concern is the mammoth absence; market should be informative.
runners.comment: Last seen in Feb 2024 when beating notable opposition in a Grade 2 at Leopardstown

runners.name: The Old Head
runners.spotlight: The clearcut winner of a Dromahane point in early December (soft) and has joined a good yard for rules action; any market confidence should be noted.
runners.comment: The clearcut winner of a Dromahane point in early December (soft)

runners.name: Luidgi Du Luy
runners.spotlight: Well-held third behind useful opposition in a French hurdle; it wasn't pretty when winning at Navan but the ground was heavy and he got the job done at odds of 4-1; open to further improvement and the recent rain is welcome.
runners.comment: It wasn't pretty when winning at Navan but the ground was heavy and he got the job done

runners.name: Queen Alice
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners Queens Gamble (useful bumper and 1m7f-2m hurdle/chase) and Brosna Queen (bumper); dam bumper, 1m6f/1m7f Flat (RPR 85) and useful 2m-2m1f hurdle/chase winner; bred to have a future but this is no easy starter.
runners.comment: Well related mare so she's bred to have a future, but this is no easy starter
runners.owner: Mrs A Frost & Edward Galvin & R H Fowler

runners.name: Leonard Vallis
runners.spotlight: Second in a maiden hurdle on debut; flopped in his first bumper but won the second at Thurles on soft ground; this race is considerably stronger than that one.
runners.comment: Showed a good attitude to win at Thurles but this race is considerably stronger

runners.name: Dorie Miller
runners.spotlight: No closer than 14l in four bumpers for Noel Meade and was tailed off in another on debut for current yard; instantly opposable; tongue tied.
runners.comment: Minor form in five bumpers and instantly opposable

runners.name: Big Boy Dan
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to 2m4f hurdle/chase winner Exit To The West; dam point winner, half-sister to useful 2m7f-3m2f hurdle/chase winner Potters Cross; asked a stiff question on what's a belated debut at the age of seven.
runners.comment: Asked a stiff question on what's a belated debut at the age of seven

runners.name: Octo Gane
runners.spotlight: Raced too freely and managed only fourth of eight when 6-4 favourite for debut at Huntingdon (good) in May 2024; probably a nice prospect but this is a big ask after so long out.
runners.comment: Too free when a beaten favourite on Huntingdon debut nearly two years ago
runners.owner: Lynne Maclennan & Les De La Haye

runners.name: Storm Queen
runners.spotlight: Flat-bred filly who won a 3m point on good to yielding and perhaps resented the heavy ground when nowhere in a Limerick bumper; still has a lot to prove in a race of this nature.
runners.comment: Point winner who perhaps resented the heavy ground when nowhere in a Limerick bumper

races.name: Leisure Dome Ltd. Marquee Hire Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Oaks Soldier
runners.spotlight: Just denied in a handicap over this trip at Thurles last month which was his best run so far; Killarney yard won this two years ago and has had a few others run well lately.
runners.comment: Just denied in a handicap over this trip at Thurles and yard won this two years ago

runners.name: Nopresentliketime
runners.spotlight: Good staying pedigree and was an expensive store; promise in sole bumper and bumped into a good one when second on hurdling debut at Ayr (2m4f, soft); weakened tamely in 2m6f contest on heavy last time.
runners.comment: Bumped into a good one when second on hurdling debut at Ayr (2m4f, soft); poor last time

runners.name: Strange Currency
runners.spotlight: Form of his third over extended 2m3f at Punchestown has worked out very well (second, fourth and fifth all won next time) but he disappointed last time over just short of 2m5f; plenty of stamina in pedigree, so this test might suit.
runners.comment: Form of his third over extended 2m3f at Punchestown  worked out well but poor effort since

runners.name: Belladona Princess
runners.spotlight: Third of 20 at Naas (2m3f) on her second start; raised 5lb after finishing second over 3m at Limerick last month where her jumping was ragged; new headgear.
runners.comment: Up 5lb after finishing second over 3m at Limerick where her jumping was ragged; headgear

runners.name: Jackmoon
runners.spotlight: Had some very useful bumper form; distant second on hurdling debut (2m4f) in the autumn; bounced back from poor effort when fifth over 2m4f here last month, but was beaten 20l; should come on a bit for that run and this is easier.
runners.comment: Bounced back from poor effort when fifth over 2m4f here last month and this is easier

runners.name: Fenian Maxios
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in first two starts over around 2m4f before a much-improved second to 120-rated rival at Thurles (2m); not much behind him that day, though, and stamina to prove (dam did win a point).
runners.comment: Second to 120-rated rival at Thurles (2m); not much behind him that day, though

runners.name: Brightmarchmorning
runners.spotlight: Brother to useful 1m7f-2m1f hurdle/chase winner Vanderpoel; dam unraced half-sister to winners Ballinhassig (3m1f chase) and Gentle Alice (useful 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase); E18,000 3yo; nice page but best watched.
runners.comment: Brother to useful 1m7f-2m1f hurdle/chase winner Vanderpoel; E18,000 3yo; best watched

runners.name: Tom Mac
runners.spotlight: Brother to yard's point/3m hurdle winner Rebel Budz was retained at the sales a couple of times; dam unraced sister to useful 2m/2m1f hurdle winner Hearts Are Trumps, out of unraced sister to Cheltenham Gold Cup winner Denman; keep an eye on the market.
runners.comment: Brother to yard's point/3m hurdle winner Rebel Budz; keep an eye on the market

runners.name: Walk Away Harry
runners.spotlight: Had smart form in his younger days when with Charles Byrnes, but has regressed considerably; starts off with new yard now.
runners.comment: Smart form in his younger days when with Charles Byrnes, but has regressed considerably

runners.name: Bruise Hill
runners.spotlight: Nothing so far in two points and when 200-1 on hurdling debut here last month.
runners.comment: Nothing so far in two points and when 200-1 on hurdling debut here last month

runners.name: Mr Babes
runners.spotlight: Nothing to recommend him so far ahead of yard/hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Nothing to recommend him so far ahead of yard/hurdling debut.

runners.name: Friends Of Barry
runners.spotlight: Respected yard and cost E52,000 as a store but beaten 49l in both starts over 2m; not really bred for this longer trip (related to 7f/1m winners).
runners.comment: Respected yard and cost E52,000 as a store but beaten 49l in both starts over 2m

runners.name: Three County Star
runners.spotlight: Modest handicapper tries longer trip but can't be fancied.
runners.comment: Modest handicapper tries longer trip but can't be fancied
runners.owner: John P Coleman & M J Caffrey

races.name: Finbarr Quaid Electrical Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Tea It Is
runners.spotlight: Maiden has had plenty of chances but was placed four times (once here) in good-ground contests during a consistent spell of form last summer; returns from nearly six months off.
runners.comment: Placed four times (once here) in good-ground contests last summer; off nearly six months

runners.name: Party Pack
runners.spotlight: Maiden's best effort was when second at Wexford (2m1f, good) in August; not seen since being pulled up on heavy ground in November.
runners.comment: Maiden's best effort was when second at Wexford (2m1f, good) in August; back from a break

runners.name: Whiskey Serenade
runners.spotlight: Point winner last year and placed in modest maiden hurdle at Tramore (2m5f, good); pulled up on handicap debut and then had a couple of spins over fences; off five months, but at least she's still unexposed.
runners.comment: Placed in modest maiden hurdle at Tramore (2m5f, good); pulled up on handicap debut

runners.name: Bowgate Street
runners.spotlight: Was in good form in the autumn of 2024 with a win and two seconds; not as effective last year apart from a close third at Galway in September; probably best watched after a layoff.
runners.comment: Close third at Galway in September; probably best watched after a layoff.

runners.name: Back To Cali
runners.spotlight: Maiden's best run in four handicap starts was when third of 20 at Down Royal (2m1f, yielding) at Christmas; twice well held on more testing ground since; might go okay on likely better surface here.
runners.comment: Maiden's best run handicaps was when third of 20 at Down Royal (2m1f, yielding)

runners.name: King Bob
runners.spotlight: Placed in all three completed starts in 2m handicaps, including when no match for easy winner at Naas (Deise Crystal fourth) last month; hood tried on that occasion is now left off; look yard's main hope and should be thereabouts again.
runners.comment: Placed in all three completed starts in 2m handicaps including when second at Naas

runners.name: Sunwalk
runners.spotlight: Flat winner had been disappointing over hurdles, and in a spell point-to-pointing, before being touched off at 66-1 at Down Royal (2m1f, yielding) over Christmas; pulled up over 2m4f back there last month; second runner for yard.
runners.comment: Touched off at 66-1 at Down Royal (2m1f, yielding) over Christmas; poor over 2m4f since

runners.name: Mollys Dolly
runners.spotlight: Showed up well on bumper debut; not much after until beaten 7l on handicap debut at Roscommon last July; off since.
runners.comment: Beaten 7l on handicap debut at Roscommon last July; off since.

runners.name: Let It Settle
runners.spotlight: Had a couple of nice runs in good-ground bumpers last summer; out the back in three maidens on winter ground, but she might be a different proposition in a handicap on better terrain.
runners.comment: Had a couple of nice runs in good-ground bumpers; out the back in three winter maidens

runners.name: Toon Town
runners.spotlight: Fair performer in handicap hurdles for Brendan Walsh, winning off 87 at Wexford in 2024; had five runs over fences for Gavin Cromwell, just denied in two-miler at Catterick in December; off since and new trainer fits cheekpieces insted of blinkers.
runners.comment: Fair performer in handicap hurdles; just denied in two-mile chase at Catterick in December

runners.name: Kings Son
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a point and a restricted maiden hurdle last year; well held over 2m5f in first two handicaps and drops back in trip here after a break.
runners.comment: Well held over 2m5f in first two handicaps and drops back in trip here after a break

runners.name: Deise Crystal
runners.spotlight: Went well for a long way on handicap debut at Naas when fourth to a well-treated winner; 5lb better off with more exposed runner-up King Bob; leading contender.
runners.comment: Went well for a long way on handicap debut at Naas when fourth to a well-treated winner

runners.name: Ranting And Raven
runners.spotlight: Blinkered when placed three times in middle-distance Flat handicaps on a sound surface for Johnny Murtagh; had her three qualifying runs in the autumn and returns from a break for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Blinkered when placed three times in middle-distance Flat handicaps; handicap hurdle debut

runners.name: Euro Maker
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; not shown much so far and 50-1 when pulled up on handicap bow in December (2m4f).
runners.comment: Not shown much so far and 50-1 when pulled up on handicap bow in December (2m4f); reserve

runners.name: Brave Endeavour
runners.spotlight: Point winner on heavy has returned from long layoff this year; beaten 23l on recent handicap debut at Clonmel; drops in trip and tongue-tie goes on.
runners.comment: Beaten 23l on recent handicap debut at Clonmel; drops in trip and tongue-tie goes on

runners.name: Jurality
runners.spotlight: Fair maiden handicapper on the level and he's fit from the all-weather; handicap debut in this sphere and he caught the eye in a good novice event at Galway last summer; may want better ground but not ruled out.
runners.comment: Handicap debut in this sphere and he caught the eye in a good novice event at Galway

runners.name: The Sugarhill Lady
runners.spotlight: Ran okay in maidens but has come down 13lb from initial handicap mark; her latest sixth at Limerick's Christmas meeting was a fair effort.
runners.comment: Has come down 13lb from initial handicap mark; latest sixth at Limerick was a fair effort
runners.owner: Eamon Leahy & Seamus ORiordan & Carmel Walsh

runners.name: Houndsworth
runners.spotlight: First reserve; ordinary Flat form in Britain but trainer spent Â£21,000 to get him last summer; quiet runs in maidens; tongue tied for handicap debut after a break.
runners.comment: Quiet runs in maidens; tongue-tied for handicap debut after a break; reserve

runners.name: Topothelane
runners.spotlight: Point winner has shown moderate form over hurdles; has obviously had his issues and makes handicap debut after best part of two years off.
runners.comment: Point winner has shown moderate form over hurdles; handicap debut after long absence

runners.name: Kilt
runners.spotlight: Winning miler at three for William Haggas and still holds rating of 85 on the level; quiet over hurdles so far and back from a break; trainer also has one of the form horses in this in the shape of King Bob.
runners.comment: Winning miler at three for William Haggas and still rated 85 on Flat; quiet over hurdles

races.name: Coolmore N.H. Sires Kyprios Handicap Chase

runners.name: Path dOroux
runners.spotlight: Useful although hard to win with and soundly beaten lately, but the handicappper is relenting; one of three runners for in-form yard
runners.comment: Hard to win with and soundly beaten lately, but the handicappper is relenting

runners.name: Glenashling
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner and useful novice form over fences this term; looked like 2m4f stretched him when last seen at Christmas; has his chance on handicap bow with leading claimer on board.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner and useful novice form over fences; has his chance on handicap bow

runners.name: Prince Of Air
runners.spotlight: Likes a sharp 2m and good ground; first past the post against useful rival at Killarney in October and better for recent comeback; 2lb wrong and ground may not be dry enough.
runners.comment: First past the post against useful rival at Killarney in October and better for comeback

runners.name: River Vale
runners.spotlight: Has been in good form at a lower level in both codes this winter; in front when falling at the last at Thurles (2m2f); beaten 10l over that trip last time and cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: In front when falling at the last at Thurles (2m2f); held last time and cheekpieces added

runners.name: Sky Lord
runners.spotlight: Won his maiden hurdle here and some useful novice chase form; heavily backed last time in a rated novice at Down Royal where he flattened out after travelling well, and yard in better form now.
runners.comment: Won maiden hurdle here and some usfel novice chase form; heavily backed last time

runners.name: Champagne Mahler
runners.spotlight: Won valauble Gowran handicap last year and good second at Naas in January; poor effort last time.
runners.comment: Won valuable Gowran handicap last year and good second at Naas in January; poor last time

races.name: Rathbarry & Glenview Studs Mares Maiden Hunters Chase

runners.name: Dceilingcantholdus
runners.spotlight: Placed in her two starts in points last month; this looks too demanding at an early stage of her career.
runners.comment: Placed in her two starts in points, this looks demanding at an early stage of her career

runners.name: Tiffinys Girl
runners.spotlight: Placed in a maiden hurdle last season; has made the frame in all four outings in points; likely to find a few too good.
runners.comment: Has made the frame in all four outings in points, likely to find a few too good

runners.name: Lucy Wang
runners.spotlight: Has won twice in points this season; most recent success was in the prestigious Gain Mares Final at Ballynoe for this rider; useful sort but well held by The Great Unknown on running at Ballycahane.
runners.comment: Has won twice in points this season, held by The Great Unknown on running at Ballycahane

runners.name: Rebel Treaty
runners.spotlight: Ordinary hurdles form, three wins in points; has to bounce back from a poor recent run at Knockanohill; did well to beat The Great Unknown's stablemate Shake The Bag in a match at Ballyvodock.
runners.comment: Ordinary hurdles form, three wins in points, has to bounce back from a poor recent run

races.name: John Thomas McNamara Series Maiden Hunters Chase

runners.name: Sampoet
runners.spotlight: Only one win from 16 starts in points, pulled up in two races last October; same outcome in a hunter chase at Thurles in February; only 1 1/2l behind Focus Point when third at Down Royal recently; this race looks stronger; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Only 1 1/2l behind Focus Point when third at Down Royal recently, this race looks stronger

runners.name: Focus Point
runners.spotlight: Seven wins in points; had been off the track for a long time before last month's second in a hunter chase at Down Royal; good prospects of confirming form with Sampoet.
runners.comment: Seven wins in points, best form in 2023 but his recent Down Royal second was encouraging

runners.name: Nibbles
runners.spotlight: Placed in a hunter chase at Limerick in December; held by Dromleigh on the evidence of a point-to-point clash at Ballycahane.
runners.comment: Placed in a hunter chase at Limerick in December, held by Dromleigh on point-to-point form

runners.name: Dromleigh
runners.spotlight: Has won his three starts in points since pulled up in a hunter chase at Clonmel in January; made the favourite Hearts And Spades work hard for his Dromahane win, and may again give his younger rival most to do.
runners.comment: Has won his last three starts in points since second to Hearts And Spades at Dromahane

runners.name: Begger
runners.spotlight: Has won only one of his ten starts in points; weak form since Moig South maiden win in the autumn campaign; unlikely to make an impact in this grade.
runners.comment: Has won only one of his ten starts in points, unlikely to make an impact in this grade

runners.name: Bens Baby
runners.spotlight: Beaten 13l when second in a maiden point last month; faces a stiff test on racecourse debut; has cheekpieces and a tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Beaten 13l when second in a maiden point last month, faces a stiff test

runners.name: Atlantian
runners.spotlight: Placed over hurdles at a modest level in 2023; recent point winner for this rider at Knockanohill; a lot more on his plate here.
runners.comment: Placed over hurdles at a modest level in 2023, recent point winner, more on his plate here

runners.name: Lawful Ruler
runners.spotlight: Has won only once from ten starts in points; produced a career-best in defeat against West Of Idaho at Tyrella last month; limited encouragement from a recent remote third in a hunter chase at Down Royal.
runners.comment: Modest record in points, limited encouragement from a recent remote third at Down Royal

runners.name: Hearts And Spades
runners.spotlight: Outstanding form in points since October, winning six of his seven races, including a hard-earned victory over Dromleigh at Dromahane in December; his winning run came to an end at Portrush nine days ago; no disgrace in losing out by a neck to Winged Leader, the most prolific Irish points winner in history (37 wins).
runners.comment: Six consecutive wins in points before a narrow defeat on latest, holds a leading chance

runners.name: Occupational Force
runners.spotlight: Reasonably consistent in points; one win and nine places from 12 starts; remote last of five finishers at Gowran a month ago on first hunter chase start.
runners.comment: Reasonably consistent in points, remote last of five finishers on only hunter chase start

runners.name: Our Story
runners.spotlight: Remote second at 33-1 in a hunter chase at Thurles in January; overall points form is unconvincing.
runners.comment: Remote second at 33-1 in a hunter chase at Thurles, overall points form is unconvincing

runners.name: Pirate Island
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden over hurdles; points form not strong enough to suggest he can feature.
runners.comment: 14-race maiden over hurdles, points form not strong enough to suggest he can feature

runners.name: Hunt Away
runners.spotlight: Winner of three points; has failed to add to his tally this season; has been keeping good company but others have stronger claims.
runners.comment: Winner of three points, has failed to add to his tally this season, others preferred

runners.name: The Happy Huntsman
runners.spotlight: Won a four-runner point in March; tailed off behind Dromleigh at Knockanohill on his most recent outing.
runners.comment: Won a four-runner point in March, tailed off behind Dromleigh on his most recent outing

runners.name: Robin Elite
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner last May; in good hands but has been struggling in low-grade handicaps, up against it.
runners.comment: In good hands but has been struggling in low-grade handicaps, up against it

runners.name: Tea In The Park
runners.spotlight: Weak racecourse form and has failed to win in points; unlikely to feature.
runners.comment: Weak racecourse form and has failed to win in points, unlikely to feature

runners.name: Pure Decent
runners.spotlight: Not much mileage on the clock; lacks a recent outing; very hard to make a case for him.
runners.comment: Not much mileage on the clock, lacks a recent outing, very hard to make a case for him

races.name: Jack Tyner Memorial Hunters Chase

runners.name: Solitary Man
runners.spotlight: Useful track performer at best with 3-4 record in points since last October; capable of better than he showed when fourth in a hunter chase run at Thurles in January; subsequently beat record-breaking pointer Winged Leader at Nenagh, with Whacker Clan a distant third; sights lowered after Cheltenham Festival bid.
runners.comment: Useful track performer with 3-4 record in points, sights lowered after Cheltenham run
runners.owner: Shanahan/Mrs M V Magnier/Mrs Carthy/Mrs Bartlett

runners.name: Asian Master
runners.spotlight: Promising sort early in his career for this stable and initially did well for Willie Mullins, winning twice over hurdles and taking fourth in the Supreme Novices'; also placed in good company over fences; has prospered again in points this season and looks the part here.
runners.comment: Smart hurdler who has also kept good company over fences, good form in points this term

runners.name: Journey With Me
runners.spotlight: Achieved a high standard in his prime for Henry de Bromhead; three wins over fences, including at Grade 2/3 level; unconvincing form in points in recent months, beaten favourite in three of his last four races.
runners.comment: Achieved a high standard in his prime for Henry de Bromhead, unconvincing form in points

runners.name: Bartlemy Boy
runners.spotlight: Four wins and three seconds from ten starts in points; won a hunter chase at Killarney last May; started the autumn campaign with second behind Cheltenham Festival winner Wonderwall at Dromahane; won at Ballindenisk in November; fair chance if forgiven a poor Thurles run; rider provides good value for his 7lb claim
runners.comment: Fair chance if forgiven a poor Thurles run, rider provides good value for his 7lb claim

runners.name: In Excelsis Deo
runners.spotlight: Solid overall form in points, highlighted by a defeat of Its On The Line at Cragmore in January; unseated rider in Belclare contest won by Asian Master; stablemate Solitary Man has superior claims.
runners.comment: Solid overall form in points, unseated rider in Belclare contest won by Asian Master

runners.name: Jetoile
runners.spotlight: Very useful hurdler/chaser in Britain in his prime; no appeal on his points form, which has been poor since a 12l second behind good yardstick Lifetime Ambition at Turtulla in November.
runners.comment: Very useful hurdler/chaser in Britain in his prime, no appeal on this season's points form

runners.name: Whacker Clan
runners.spotlight: Very useful form for Henry de Bromhead earlier in his career, three chase wins and third in the Kim Muir in 2024; modest points form lately.
runners.comment: Very useful form for Henry de Bromhead earlier in his career, modest points form lately

runners.name: Run Wild Fred
runners.spotlight: Troytown winner in 2021 and second in the National Hunt Chase later that season; has not been a force in recent seasons; pulled up in a point in January; risky at this stage.
runners.comment: Troytown winner in 2021, has not been a force in recent seasons, risky at this stage

runners.name: Carrig Wells
runners.spotlight: Very experienced pointer, two wins and often placed; makes little appeal in this company at this stage of her career.
runners.comment: Very experienced pointer, two wins and often placed, makes little appeal in this company

runners.name: Galon De Vauzelle
runners.spotlight: Won a soft-ground point last autumn; pulled up in two Irish hunter chases this season; remote fourth at Carlisle last month.
runners.comment: Pulled up in two Irish hunter chases this season, remote fourth at Carlisle last month
runners.owner: J P Lane & Emma Downey & P J Rothwell

runners.name: Temptationinmilan
runners.spotlight: More than three years since his last racecourse win; huge price when pulled up in a hunter chase at Thurles in January; unlikely to end his losing sequence.
runners.comment: More than three years since his last racecourse win, unlikely to end his losing sequence

runners.name: Battle Of Benburb
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler at a modest level; struggling in point-to-points this season.
runners.comment: Winning hurdler at a modest level, struggling in point-to-point this season

races.name: Pegus Horse Feed Point-To-Point Flat Race

runners.name: Boundfornowhere
runners.spotlight: Made E70,000 as 3yo; closely related to bumper/2m4f hurdle winner Tarras Wood, half-brother to winners Emily Moon (useful bumper and 2m-2m2f hurdle/chase) and Scented Lilly (2m7f hurdle); dam 2m hurdle winner; Ballindenisk winner in January for Tom Keating and starts off here for top yard.
runners.comment: Ballindenisk winner in January for Tom Keating and is related to bumper winners

runners.name: Sharp As Glass
runners.spotlight: From a top pointing yard and has won twice on soft this winter; the only one of these to have run under rules, and he performed well when second to a useful type in Killarney bumper last May; tongue-tie added to hood.
runners.comment: From a top pointing yard and has won twice since good second in a bumper last May

runners.name: Clais Mhor Capri
runners.spotlight: Dam bumper and 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner, closely related to bumper winner Miss Doyenne, out of unraced half-sister to smart bumper and 2m-3m2f hurdle/chase winner Battlecry; narrowly beaten at Kildorrery and early casualty when odds-on at Ballyknock last week.
runners.comment: Narrowly beaten at Kildorrery and early casualty when odds-on last week; dam won bumper

runners.name: Flamm De Kerbarh
runners.spotlight: Made E27,000 as 3yo; half-brother to French 2m4f-2m7f cross-country chase winner Reve De Fee; dam French 1m3f winner, half-sister to useful bumper and 2m/2m3f hurdle winner Authorised Speed, out of French 1m4f/12.5f Listed winner; beaten 5l when third at Belclare last month.
runners.comment: Beaten 5l when third at Belclare last month; dam won over 1m3f in France

runners.name: Kill Vanhowe
runners.spotlight: Made E24,000 as 2yo; half-brother to bumper/2m4f hurdle winner Kajikia; dam twice-raced French 2m1f hurdle winner, half-sister to French winners Kanaeva (2m2f hurdle), Han Shan (1m4f) and Polynikova (15.5f/2m1f); off the mark on second start at Oldtown in February.
runners.comment: Half-brother to bumper winner; off the mark on second start at Oldtown in February

runners.name: Pros And Cons
runners.spotlight: By Frankel's brother Proconsul; dam unplaced in bumper/2m4f hurdle, out of unraced half-sister to high-class staying jumper Cab On Target and smart 2m-3m jumper Glenelly Gale; beaten 30l by Lair Du Laube on his debut at Comea.
runners.comment: Beaten 30l by Lair Du Laube on his debut at Comea; family of Cab On Target

runners.name: Royal Mal
runners.spotlight: Malinas half-brother to 2m/2m2f hurdle winner Get The Value; dam multiple Italian jumps winner out of Italian 7f winner, family of Italian Oaks winner Maria Waleska; 7.5l third to Kill Vanhowe at Oldtown in February.
runners.comment: 7.5l third to Kill Vanhowe at Oldtown in February; half-brother to 2m/2m2f hurdle winner

runners.name: Zambezi Moon
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to point winner; dam point winner, half-sister to useful 3m/3m1f chase winner Glencairn View, 2m1f/2m7f hurdle winner Flemerina and bumper/2m4f hurdle winner Werenearlyoutofit; well beaten when third in first two points; looks an outsider.
runners.comment: Well beaten when third in first two points; looks an outsider

races.name: Cork & Waterford Mares Point-To-Point Flat Race

runners.name: Tintara
runners.spotlight: Well-related mare fell in her first two point-to-points; bought for 55,000GBP after a second-place finish in a mares' maiden at Ballycrystal; has joined a top yard and this half-sister to Tombstone has to be on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Well related mare, bought for 55,000GBP after Ballycrystal second, shortlisted

runners.name: Born Believing
runners.spotlight: Won a mares' maiden on debut on soft ground at Belharbour in February when only three finished; from a family that her trainer has had plenty of success with; could go well; tongue tied.
runners.comment: Won mares' maiden at Belharbour on sole start, related to Rules' winners, could go well

runners.name: Lucky Valley
runners.spotlight: 1-8 in point-to-points, the win coming on her seventh start at Knockanard (3m, soft); 3.5l runner-up behind odds-on favourite in a winners-of-one at Durrow on latest last month; she's a player.
runners.comment: 1-8 in points, won at Knockanard on penultimate, decent second latest, player

runners.name: Wilton Mills Trier
runners.spotlight: Won on debut in a mares' maiden at Dromahane and the third has won since; dam a half-sister to a bumper winner and leading form claims on rules debut.
runners.comment: Won sole point start at Dromahane, third has won since, leading form claims

runners.name: Julie Liath
runners.spotlight: Veteran placed in five of her six point-to-points; not disgraced when staying on late for an 8.5l sixth in a 2m2f Tipperary bumper (gd-yld) last May; place claims.
runners.comment: Veteran placed in five of six points, not disgraced in bumper last year, place shout

runners.name: Free A Minute
runners.spotlight: 0-5 in point-to-points and yet to trouble the judge; dam related to bumper winners but others have stronger form claims.
runners.comment: 0-5 in points and yet to trouble the judge, dam related to bumper winners, others stronger

runners.name: Rachel Lopez
runners.spotlight: Beaten cumulative distance of 70l in first two point-to-points and pulled up at Ballyknock just five days ago; hard to make a case for her on form.
runners.comment: Heavy defeats both completed points, pulled up latest, hard to fancy

runners.name: Jessie Roe
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in three previous bumper runs and similar story in a Belclare point-to-point on latest; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: No impact in three previous bumpers or in point on latest, hard to fancy

races.name: BetGoodwin Download The App Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Local Derby
runners.spotlight: Good-ground 3m point winner who scored at Wincanton (2m4f, heavy) in January on second handicap start, and after an early unseat at Fontwell (jumped path) he returned to winning ways at Exeter (2m7f, good to soft) three weeks ago; this progressive 6yo has leading claims up 6lb for last time.
runners.comment: Progressive 6yo who has won his last two completed starts and holds leading claims

runners.name: All Authorized
runners.spotlight: He's had a disappointing season, initially over fences and more recently over hurdles, and has something to prove; however, he's now 10lb lower than when winning over C&D (good) last April and can't be written off; monitor the betting.
runners.comment: Disappointing season but now 10lb lower than when winning over C&D last April

runners.name: The Wise Traveller
runners.spotlight: 10yo who has finished merely seventh on his last three starts, the latest in January; however, a break may have helped and he was returning from some time off when winning under Jamie Gambin over C&D (good) last September; now 3lb lower than for that win and not discounted.
runners.comment: Below par when last seen but break may have helped; C&D win off 3lb higher last September
