runners.name: Mild Magic
runners.spotlight: Hasn't been able to get seriously competitive across her five rules runs, in handicaps on last two occasions, but at least her 13l third here (2m4f, soft) in February was a step in the right direction; this 3m point runner-up (good to yielding) could build on it now up in trip and on better ground.
runners.comment: Step in right direction here last time and the longer trip/better ground may help today

runners.name: Hilltown
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D in April 2023 and a chase here in April 2024; however, he's struggled this season following a long absence, including at this course on three occasions; 4lb out of the handicap; major turnaround needed.
runners.comment: Two-time course winner but he's struggled this season (including here) after long absence

runners.name: Barrow Ranger
runners.spotlight: He's hard to fancy on his rules form and was soundly beaten over fences last time, but he won a point-to-point two starts ago and this 5yo Irish challenger is one to watch in the betting.
runners.comment: Irish raider; hard to fancy on rules form but won a point two starts ago; watch the market

races.name: BetGoodwin Sussex Champion Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Kotmask
runners.spotlight: His jumping has been an issue this season and he was soundly beaten at Newbury last time; however, two starts ago he was a close second of four there (2m, good to soft) and he has a good record at this track; might not be far away if finding some fluency.
runners.comment: Jumping issues this term but has good record here; may not be far away if finding fluency

runners.name: Scorsese
runners.spotlight: Made all at Sedgefield (2m3f, good to soft) on Boxing Day on fourth chase start and his good form has continued when third on both runs since (2m4f, soft/good to soft); this drop back in trip is worth a go and further improvement is possible, but he needs to be better than ever from 4lb wrong.
runners.comment: In good form over 2m3f/2m4f; this trip could suit but he's 4lb wrong at the weights

runners.name: Secret Des Dieux
runners.spotlight: Capitalised on the drop back in trip to win at Huntingdon (2m, heavy) in February and followed up at Kempton (2m2f, good); he's 7lb higher than last time and in a hotter race, but did it very nicely and this 6yo novice is now 3-6 over fences; could still have more to offer and he's respected.
runners.comment: 6yo novice; did it very nicely at Kempton last month to make it 3-6 over fences; respected

runners.name: Edwardstone
runners.spotlight: 12yo whose career highlight came when winning the Tingle Creek in 2022; showed he retains considerable spark when winning Grade 2 at Kempton (2m4f, good) this January; he may prefer a longer trip than this nowadays but he's a leading player nevertheless now back in a handicap.
runners.comment: 12yo who showed with Grade 2 win in January he retains considerable spark; leading player

runners.name: Matterhorn
runners.spotlight: Made it 5-9 over fences with convincing win at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good) last May; pulled up on sole run since, at Ascot in December, but the good to soft ground may not have suited; second in this last year; could go well.
runners.comment: Won in good style at Uttoxeter last May; pulled up on sole run since but could bounce back

races.name: Roy Reeve Memorial Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Loriko
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on handicap debut at Hereford (2m3f) in December when soft going shouldn't have been an issue (ran well on testing ground in France); however, he ran really well in defeat in the autumn on his first three British starts (2m-2m3f, good) and will be tough to beat if rediscovering that form.
runners.comment: Well beaten on handicap debut at Hereford but tough to beat if rediscovering earlier form
runners.owner: Cherry Knoll Farm M Ward & D Skelton

runners.name: Answer That
runners.spotlight: Showed promise when making the frame in two bumpers in Ireland (soft to heavy/yielding); however, tailed off on his first two hurdle starts (2m, soft/good to soft) and needs to be an entirely different proposition today.
runners.comment: Showed promise in bumpers in Ireland but tailed off on first two hurdle starts

runners.name: Oblemenko
runners.spotlight: Brother to bumper/hurdle winner Sansrick (RPR 121); soundly beaten in both Irish maiden points; one of two for stable that has good record in this race; market check advised but others have far more compelling credentials.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in both points; check market but others have more compelling credentials

runners.name: Flash Man
runners.spotlight: Two-time French Flat winner who was a promising third at Kempton (2m, good) a fortnight ago on his second hurdle start; open to further improvement in this code and can give another good account.
runners.comment: 2 Flat wins in France; 3rd at Kempton on second hurdle run; can give another good account

runners.name: Tetsworth
runners.spotlight: 0-8 on the Flat but has shown a fair level of ability in that code, including on last two starts; shaped when fourth of nine on hurdle debut at Kempton (2m, good) in December as though he would improve for that experience; stable has strong record in race; he could be the main threat to Loriko.
runners.comment: 0-8 on the Flat but with ability; encouraging fourth on sole hurdle start and can improve

races.name: Southern Cranes & Access Ltd Novices' Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Tennessee Tango
runners.spotlight: Good-ground bumper runner-up who improved with each of his first four runs over hurdles, easily winning a maiden here (2m, soft) in February; pulled up in the competitive EBF Final at Sandown (2m4f, soft) since and needs to prove his stamina, but pedigree provides hope; not written off.
runners.comment: Pulled up in competitive EBF Final at Sandown but progressive previously; not written off

runners.name: Grand Conqueror
runners.spotlight: Won on handicap debut over C&D (good to soft) in December and followed up at Fontwell (2m1f, good to soft) 11 days later, before clear second to well-handicapped winner at Kempton (2m5f, good to soft) in February; he's up 4lb for last time but the form reads very well and he could settle better in a first-time hood; lots to like.
runners.comment: 2-2 in handicaps before coming clear with well-treated winner last time; lots to like

runners.name: Maldini Milano
runners.spotlight: Good-ground bumper winner who got off the mark over hurdles at the third attempt over C&D (good) in November, when wearing first-time cheekpieces (retained) in a maiden that worked out extremely well; weakened into sixth on handicap debut at Windsor in January but that was on soft ground; firmly in calculations now back on a better surface.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner; weakened on handicap debut but on soft; firmly in calculations

runners.name: Minella Jury
runners.spotlight: Irish bumper winner (yielding) who followed up on hurdle/stable debut at Uttoxeter (2m, heavy) in December, and he was disputing the lead and clear of the rest when falling at the final flight on handicap debut at Doncaster (2m, good to soft) last time; the winner has followed up off 6lb higher so a 4lb rise for this fellow may well prove lenient; every chance he'll stay.
runners.comment: Disputing lead when final-flight faller at Doncaster and the winner followed up

runners.name: Mount Anglesby
runners.spotlight: 7yo who has a fair bit of experience but has been progressive for a new yard this term, making it two wins from his last three runs (2m3f/2m5f, good/good to soft) when scoring at Warwick last time; entitled to respect.
runners.comment: Progressive for new yard this term and has won two of his last three starts; respected

runners.name: Easy Love
runners.spotlight: Course bumper winner who easily made all on handicap debut at Windsor (2m4f, good) on New Year's Day; unplaced/pulled up in Grade 2/Listed races on heavy/soft going on her two starts since but she could be an entirely different proposition now back in a handicap and back on better ground.
runners.comment: Easily made all at Windsor in January on last handicap start and she's one to consider

runners.name: The Boss Bear
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper at Fontwell (2m1f, good) last May and followed up on hurdle debut over C&D (good to soft) in December; however, he's been pulled up/well beaten in his first two handicaps and needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Won on hurdle debut over C&D in December but pulled up/well beaten in his two handicaps

runners.name: Kel Du Large
runners.spotlight: Listed bumper winner who scored at Hereford (2m3f, heavy) in February on his third handicap start and was a close second to Mount Anglesby (2m5f, good to soft) four weeks ago; this 6yo could continue to progress now things are starting to click; possible contender in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Listed bumper winner for whom things are clicking over hurdles; possible contender

runners.name: Jefe Triunfo
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle at Warwick (2m, good) last June and has dropped down the weights, but he didn't show enough in handicaps in the autumn to have pressing claims today; returns from a break.
runners.comment: Didn't show enough in handicaps in the autumn to have pressing claims today

runners.name: Percy Shelley
runners.spotlight: Useful Flat performer who made good start to hurdling career in the autumn, including when going close in a Cheltenham novice (2m4f, good to soft; has won on good ground); flopped at Ascot (2m5f, good) in November but has had a break since and retains potential now in his first handicap hurdle.
runners.comment: Useful on Flat; good start to hurdling career; pulled up last time but retains potential

runners.name: Religous Cross
runners.spotlight: Point winner who scored on his hurdle debut at Taunton (2m3f, good) in December; 28l fourth of 15 in Kempton novice (2m5f, good to soft) since but this unexposed 6yo makes his handicap debut off what looks a fair mark and he could have more to offer.
runners.comment: Just four runs, with wins in a point/novice hurdle; unexposed and could have more to offer

runners.name: High Fibre
runners.spotlight: Began to look exposed in the second half of last year but he showed improved form to win at Kempton (2m, good) on March 23 and was short-headed at Ludlow (2m, good) last Monday; he should be fine now back up in trip and could again be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Improved form last month, with a win and a near-miss; could again be thereabouts
runners.owner: G C Stevens And Mrs C Fry

races.name: BetGoodwin Sussex National Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Largy Poet
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent and pulled up in first-time blinkers at Exeter (3m6f, heavy) in February; however, he's back in cheekpieces today in which he kept on for second of six at Wincanton (3m1f, heavy) in January; versatile ground-wise and this sort of trip may yet suit; not discounted.
runners.comment: Inconsistent and pulled up last time but back in cheekpieces today and he's not discounted

runners.name: Rivers Corner
runners.spotlight: Easy win at Wincanton (3m2f, heavy) in January and bounced back from Haydock flop to win at Exeter (3m6f, good) recently, despite a bad mistake; clearly on the up and his stable has won two of the last six runnings.
runners.comment: He's won two of his last three, most recently over 3m6f; clearly on the up

runners.name: Sea Invasion
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row here (3m1f, good) last spring to take his Plumpton record to 3-4; rallied for close third of five at Kempton (3m, good) in January, hinting this first crack at a marathon trip could be a good move, especially around here; soundly beaten at Ascot last time but over hurdles.
runners.comment: This first crack at a marathon trip could be a good move, especially here where he's 3-4

runners.name: Enjoy dAllen
runners.spotlight: Three wins in small fields this season (2m5f-3m5f, good/soft) and another good run when second of three at Sedgefield (3m2f, good) recently; however, there's a suspicion this 12yo will find today's race too competitive.
runners.comment: Three small-field wins this season; this 12yo may find today's race too competitive

runners.name: Transmission
runners.spotlight: Tapped back into last season's promising form, and then some, to win by 12l in a first-time visor at Musselburgh (3m7f, good to soft; Edinburgh National) in February; has also won on good ground and 2-2 over hurdles here; an 8lb rise may not stop him if the visor maintains its positive effect.
runners.comment: Easily won the Edinburgh National at Musselburgh and an 8lb rise may not stop him

runners.name: Havaila
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (good) last March and reappeared with creditable third of 16 at Windsor (3m4f, soft) in January; bounced back from lesser run with convincing win at Newbury (2m7f, good to soft) last month and this progressive 7yo may be able to take a 9lb rise in his stride.
runners.comment: Convincing win at Newbury last month and this progressive 7yo has the necessary stamina

runners.name: Tommie Beau
runners.spotlight: He's won here three times and was second in this race in 2024 on his last Plumpton visit; on a reduced mark but this 11yo has struggled to get competitive on his last few starts and needs to turn things around in first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Strong record here and has a reduced mark but this 11yo is hard to fancy on recent form

runners.name: Stans The Man
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row last May (3m5f/3m3f, good) and returned to form to win at Wincanton (3m1f, good to soft) last month following a break; this 9yo has never defied a mark this high but it may be within range if he's once more at the top of his game.
runners.comment: Back to form to win at Wincanton last month and his revised mark may be within range

runners.name: Planned Paradise
runners.spotlight: Exploited a reduced mark to win at Bangor (3m5f, soft) in February and again had plenty in hand at Hexham (4m, soft) 12 days ago; this is a hotter race than last time and this 10yo is up 10lb, but he's clearly in rude health and could be bang there once more; handles any ground.
runners.comment: Has won his last two starts in good style; up in the weights but clearly in rude health

runners.name: Unanswered Prayers
runners.spotlight: Won the Southern National (3m3f, good) in November 2024 and he's on a dangerous mark on form as recent as October 2025; however, this 10yo hasn't threatened since and needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: On a dangerous mark on form as recent as October but this 10yo hasn't threatened since

races.name: Gordon And Angela Ashdown Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: A Tickatickatiming
runners.spotlight: Bumper and maiden hurdle winner on this course in 2023; has run some good races in defeat here since early last spring and has a chance at the weights with Yasmina on a C&D run in February; improvement not really in the offing but she can be involved.
runners.comment: Bumper/maiden hurdle winner here in 2023; in good form and should be close up again

runners.name: Risk It All
runners.spotlight: French Flat winner in 2024; maiden hurdler who is proving hard to place in handicaps, albeit his latest defeat, over C&D last month, came at the hands of one who'd dropped a long way in the weights; needs more.
runners.comment: French Flat winner but proving hard to place over hurdles so far; needs extra

runners.name: Yasmina
runners.spotlight: Hinted at minor ability before having wind surgery and the operation seemed to help as she forged clear of A Tickatickatiming on handicap debut over C&D (soft) in February; should make a go of it off 3lb higher.
runners.comment: Had wind op before forging clear of A Tickatickatiming on handicap debut over C&D; up 3lb

runners.name: Fascinating Lips
runners.spotlight: Hurdle winner in 2022 whose latest success came in a classified event (1m2f) last August when with the Moores; minor hurdle form in the autumn and up against it for new yard after a break.
runners.comment: Hurdle winner in 2022 but minor form in the autumn and has left the Moores since

runners.name: Public Enemy
runners.spotlight: Won good-ground maiden (C&D) and novice hurdles last spring but he ran poorly on handicap debut here in between and his form at this level since hasn't borne much fruit (first-time tongue-tie latest, blinkers now added); however, he may well do better on second run back from a break with Sam Twiston-Davies taking over.
runners.comment: Won good-ground maiden/novice hurdles last spring but ordinary handicap form so far

runners.name: Galactic Jack
runners.spotlight: Useful on the Flat, won 1m4f Salisbury handicap in 2023 off a mark of 88; more off days than on in the meantime but soft ground no help on recent yard debut after nearly two years off; worth a market check off a very low mark now back on more suitable ground; cheekpieces refitted.
runners.comment: Useful Flat form in 2023; very mixed since but on lowly mark on 2nd run back from absence

runners.name: Unjeu Royal
runners.spotlight: Won Southwell bumper in February 2025; bled from the nose at Lingfield in January but has been ready to run since February and an earlier run, a promising runner-up to a useful one at Huntingdon (2m, good to soft) in November, suggests he will be competitive on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Bumper winner; promising 2m hurdle form; likely to be competitive on handicap debut

races.name: BetGoodwin Best Odds Guaranteed Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Kamille Au Kanada
runners.spotlight: First past the post in both Irish points (good to yielding/soft), disqualified on first occasion (banned substance in sample); 14l fourth at 11-1 in Listed mares' bumper at Sandown (2m, soft) on last month's rules debut; her sights are lowered today and she could play a leading role.
runners.comment: First past the post in both points; fourth in Listed mares' bumper last time; player

runners.name: Cheap Sandwiches
runners.spotlight: Promising third in sole point and runner-up on bumper debut at Warwick (2m, good to soft) in December; disappointing at Fontwell since but the ground was heavy; retains potential and could bounce back with big run.
runners.comment: Promising second on bumper debut and subsequent lesser run was on heavy ground

runners.name: Hitintheheadlines
runners.spotlight: Point winner who also showed promise when third of six on bumper debut at Ascot (1m7f, good) in November; failed to build on that when fifth of eight at Huntingdon (2m, good to soft) but he's in top hands and remains one to be interested in.
runners.comment: A touch underwhelming at Huntingdon last time but he remains one to be interested in

runners.name: Mint Approved
runners.spotlight: One win from four point starts and ran well from the front when back in a bumper and runner-up over C&D (good to soft) last month; others have stronger form but he's open to improvement, especially if settling better.
runners.comment: 1-4 in points and runner-up in C&D bumper last month; open to improvement

races.name: Virgin Bet Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Creative Queen
runners.spotlight: $300,000 foal, $525,000 yearling, 800,000euros breeze-up 2yo; 4-9 for her Newcastle debut (6f, Tapeta) last September, but looked green when finishing second of the three runners; likely she is capable of much better.
runners.comment: Green when second of three on her Newcastle debut; likely she is capable of much better

runners.name: Rogue Messiah
runners.spotlight: Looked a nice prospect when making a successful debut at Windsor (6f, good) last August, but proved awkward when finishing in midfield at Thirsk (6f, good) the following month; gelded since; half-brother to an AW winner and respected on that debut effort from a good draw, despite the penalty.
runners.comment: Made impressive winning debut as 2yo, but proved awkward next time; player on first effort

runners.name: Zain Primus
runners.spotlight: Sent off 2-1 when third of five on his Newcastle debut (7f, Tapeta) in February; that wasn't a competitive contest, but the shorter trip should suit on breeding; Oisin Murphy is an interesting booking.
runners.comment: Third of five on Newcastle debut; shorter trip should suit; O Murphy interesting booking

runners.name: Wezzeer
runners.spotlight: 125,000euros foal; 280,000gns yearling; half-brother to German 6f-8.4f winner Wikinger; dam German Listed-placed 6f winner (RPR 95), half-sister to German 1m1f Listed winner Wish You Well; stable has a good record with newcomers and market support would be significant.
runners.comment: Stable has a good record with newcomers and market support would be significant

runners.name: King Cherry
runners.spotlight: 5,000euros foal, 13,000gns yearling; half-brother to winners Not Just Yet (7f; RPR 81) and Golden Furius (Spanish 6f 2yo); dam placed 6f AW (52), sister to 5f winner Kassbaan; already gelded and yard doesn't have many winning newcomers; stable also runs Accommodation.
runners.comment: Already gelded and yard doesn't have many winning newcomers

runners.name: Marengo Storm
runners.spotlight: Â£100,000 yearling; half-brother to 6f winner Bulldog Drummond (including 2yo/AW; RPR 91); dam 5f AW winner (82), half-sister to 5.5f/6f Listed winner Perdika; already gelded but stable can get them ready first time and he's well worth a market check.
runners.comment: Already gelded but stable can get them ready first time and he's well worth a market check

runners.name: Hugh
runners.spotlight: 75,000gns yearling; brother to 5f/6f winner Fondest Dream (including 2yo/US); dam unplaced 1m-1m3f (RPR 56), sister to winners Keystroke (6f Group 3) and Cosseted (1m-10.5f), half-sister to winners Indulged (1m2f), Canoodled (7f/1m) and Fondest (1m); unraced for Mick Appleby; from a successful family and stable can get them ready first time but he has an awkward draw for a newcomer.
runners.comment: Stable can get them ready first time but he has an awkward draw for a newcomer

runners.name: Menwirr
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in both starts over 7f on AW six months apart (gelded after debut); bred to be better than he has shown but may be one for handicaps after this.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in both starts over 7f on AW six months apart; may be one for handicaps

runners.name: Accommodation
runners.spotlight: 9-2 when sixth of ten on her Chelmsford debut (7f, Polytrack) in November when trained by William Haggas; has since changed hands for 6,000gns; may need a bit more time.
runners.comment: Sixth on Chelmsford debut in November when trained by William Haggas; may need more time

runners.name: Thats Random
runners.spotlight: Sent off 5-1 when sixth of ten on his Lingfield debut (7f, Polytrack) in November; form has worked out well and he's open to improvement, but apprentice-ridden on this return so may be one for another day.
runners.comment: Sixth on his Lingfield debut in November; apprentice-ridden on return; one for another day

runners.name: Dontwaryboutathing
runners.spotlight: 50-1 when last of six on her Newmarket debut (6f, good to firm) 11 months ago; lots more needed.
runners.comment: Last of six on her Newmarket debut 11 months ago; lots more needed

runners.name: Sun Path
runners.spotlight: Beat just one home in two starts over 6f here late last year; major improvement required and he has the outside stall.
runners.comment: Beat just one home in two starts over 6f here late last year; major improvement required

races.name: Virgin Bet Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (For horses In Bands C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Guesstimate
runners.spotlight: Placed in both starts in the autumn, the latest when second of 13 behind a well-backed newcomer over C&D in November; major player if returning in the same form.
runners.comment: Placed in both starts in the autumn, the latest when second of 13 over C&D; major player

runners.name: Freda
runners.spotlight: Made a most promising debut when running a subsequent winner close at Lingfield (7f, Polytrack) in January but hasn't really built on it in two subsequent starts, the latest over C&D 12 days ago (just behind Midnight Media); looks vulnerable.
runners.comment: Made a most promising debut but hasn't built on it in two subsequent starts; vulnerable

runners.name: Eastern Veil
runners.spotlight: Both starts last October, stepping up from her Southwell debut when winning narrowly at Wolverhampton (8.6f, Tapeta) 18 days later; the form of that race has received a few boosts but she will need to improve again under her 7lb penalty after 163 days off.
runners.comment: Won on her second start but will need to improve again under a penalty after 163 days off

runners.name: Irish Incentive
runners.spotlight: Has shown ability in two of her three starts, the latest when beaten a head into second of five at Southwell (7f, Tapeta) 16 days ago; will probably need to improve again if she to win this, though; stable also runs Miss Mambo.
runners.comment: Beaten a head at Southwell 16 days ago; will probably need to improve again to win this

runners.name: Tamzan
runners.spotlight: Showed ability last year, possibly her best effort when third of 12 over C&D in October, but ended up well held on nursery debut at Wolverhampton (9.5f, Tapeta) the following month; the most exposed in the field and more will be needed on this return.
runners.comment: Showed ability last year but the most exposed in the field and more will be needed

runners.name: Doodling
runners.spotlight: Backed in from 20-1 to 9-2 when fourth of 14 on her Newbury debut (6f, good to firm) last June; clearly has ability but her absence since is a concern and she isn't crying out for the extra 2f on pedigree.
runners.comment: Good fourth on Newbury debut but off for 286 days since and longer trip a question

runners.name: Barbuda Bay
runners.spotlight: Well held behind the winner when second of eight on her Lingfield debut (7f, Polytrack) last August then faced a stiff task in a valuable event at Goodwood (7f, good to soft) the following month; will need a bit more to take this.
runners.comment: Showed some promise on last August's debut and faced a stiff task next time; more needed

runners.name: Midnight Media
runners.spotlight: No show on her debut over 7f here in February but with the hood removed she caught the eye when fifth of 11 over C&D 12 days ago; could play a part if taking another step forward.
runners.comment: Caught the eye when fifth over C&D 12 days ago; could play a part if stepping forward

runners.name: Blue Samuna
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in three starts over 7f last year, albeit hinted at ability; extra furlong should suit but handicaps may see her in a better light.
runners.comment: Unplaced in three starts over 7f last year; handicaps may see her in a better light

runners.name: Miss Mambo
runners.spotlight: Both starts over 6f last June, finishing fourth of ten on her Chelmsford debut but disappointing when beaten a long way into last of 13 at Nottingham (good to firm) three weeks later; longer trip should suit on pedigree but still has enough to prove on return.
runners.comment: Two starts last year, showing promise on debut but disappointing next time; bit to prove

races.name: Virgin Bet Daily Price Boosts Magnolia Stakes (Listed Race)

runners.name: Devils Advocate
runners.spotlight: Bit of a mixed bag last year but showed some useful form, as when fourth of 11 in the Dante at York (1m2f, good to firm) in May and winning a 0-105 handicap at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft); won on Polytrack at Chelmsford as a 2yo and very much favoured by these terms; respected.
runners.comment: Showed useful form last year and won on Polytrack as a 2yo; much favoured by these terms

runners.name: Gethin
runners.spotlight: Has endured an abbreviated four-race career under Callum Rodriguez but has shown smart form, ending last year with second-place finishes in Listed events at Saint-Cloud (1m4f, very soft) and Newmarket (1m2f, good to soft); no problem with the five-month absence and is half-brother to two AW winners including Global Giant, who won this race in 2021; major player.
runners.comment: Useful form in his four-race career; half-brother to two AW winners and goes well fresh

runners.name: Plage De Havre
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo who won twice last year, the latest a handicap at Haydock (1m4f, good) in July; never out of the first two in five completed starts on the AW, but not seen in nine months and there has to be a doubt over the suitability of a sharp 1m2f like this for him now.
runners.comment: Off nine months and there has to be a doubt over the suitability of a sharp 1m2f like this

runners.name: Military Academy
runners.spotlight: Latest win came in a Listed race over 1m4f here for the Gosdens in November 2024 but he hasn't been at his best in five starts for this yard; needs a major revival.
runners.comment: Latest win came in a Listed race here but below his best in five starts for this yard

runners.name: Muddy Mooy
runners.spotlight: Unexposed mare who is 2-4 on the AW and was last seen finishing second of 13 in a Chantilly Listed contest (10.5f, soft) last July; makes her debut for her new trainer (same owner) after 260 days off but not dismissed completely if returning in the same form.
runners.comment: 2-4 on AW and runner-up in a French Listed race when last seen; not dismissed completely

runners.name: Thunder Run
runners.spotlight: Won four of his first eight starts on turf but not seen since finishing down the field in the Cambridgeshire (1m1f, good to firm) last September; also has to prove himself on the AW but remains unexposed for his age and the stable does well with its runners here.
runners.comment: Off 191 days and unraced on the AW but remains unexposed and stable does well here

runners.name: Savvy Victory
runners.spotlight: Useful handicapper on his day including on AW, but unplaced in all nine starts since reappearing a year ago and would be receiving weight from all his rivals in a handicap.
runners.comment: Unplaced in last nine starts and would be receiving weight from his rivals in a handicap

races.name: Virgin Bet Handicap (GBBplus Race)

runners.name: Topteam
runners.spotlight: Can lead; won first three turf handicaps as 3yo, at about 1m2f and 1m4f; lost little in defeat when fourth in the Shergar Cup at Ascot after but he floundered on soft ground there in September; gelded since; solid chance on turf form back on AW for yard with a strong AW strike-rate this year.
runners.comment: Improved handicapper at up to 1m4f on turf; unsuited by soft in September; contender

runners.name: Goblet Of Fire
runners.spotlight: Was a more convincing Wolverhampton winner (2m) in October than the narrow margin suggests, making it 4-5 on sand for this yard, including at 1m3f and 1m4f on this track; well held over hurdles in November but had wind operation since; by no means out of it.
runners.comment: 4-5 on AW for this yard, including at 1m3f/1m4f here; had wind op; by no means out of it

runners.name: Midnight Rumble
runners.spotlight: Sole win in 1m2f Lingfield AW handicap nearly two years ago; has been running well since back on AW of late but it's pushing it to call him well handicapped in this competitive race.
runners.comment: Sole win at 1m2f on AW in 2024; running well but little to suggest he's well treated
runners.owner: Lets Get Ready To Rumble Partnership

runners.name: Marnier
runners.spotlight: Well connected; didn't always do himself justice as a 3yo but he was a tough opponent for a good rival (now on a mark of 101) over 1m3f at Yarmouth last May and made a good visual impression when overwhelming two rivals on Lingfield AW in November; interesting on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Useful turf form at 1m3f; straightforward task on AW debut in November; first handicap run

runners.name: Rogue Impact
runners.spotlight: A 1m4f AW maiden win last spring worked out well but reality struck in Listed race and the Derby after; in more suitable company when just unable to get past the front-running Topteam at Thirsk (1m4f) in July but he wasn't the same horse again; gelded since; could go well at a price.
runners.comment: AW maiden winner; close 2nd to Topteam in July but not the same horse after; possibilities

runners.name: Bahadur
runners.spotlight: Immediate progress for switch to AW in the autumn, especially in handicaps on last two starts when a ready winner over 1m4f at Newcastle, then held off a subsequent improver at Wolverhampton (1m6f); up further 3lb; James Doyle rarely rides for yard now but 10-41 overall; can do better again.
runners.comment: 3-3 on AW, the last two in 1m4f/1m6f handicaps; up further 3lb; can improve further
runners.owner: J William Rowley & S P C Woods

runners.name: Serenity Blue
runners.spotlight: All races at 1m2f on turf, winning two novices in the first half of 2025; dropped right out on handicap debut at Glorious Goodwood (11-1) and he didn't fare much better at Newbury in September; it could go either way on AW debut but this new trip could suit; thereabouts if it does.
runners.comment: All races at 1m2f on turf; two novice wins; well held in handicaps; new trip could suit

runners.name: Incensed
runners.spotlight: A good spell last summer for new yard included a C&D win in July but she faded badly over 1m6f at Goodwood on final start; had wind surgery since but this is likely to need her best form yet on seasonal return.
runners.comment: Good spell last summer included a C&D win; tailed off on turf latest; had wind op

runners.name: Youthful King
runners.spotlight: Multiple handicap wins, the latest over 1m6f at Sandown last June; just 1lb lower now but he has no great record fresh and it's hard to see him teed up for this against unexposed rivals.
runners.comment: Latest win came over 1m6f last June; no great record fresh; hard to see him teed up today

races.name: Virgin Bet A Good Bet Fillies' Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Lady Roxby
runners.spotlight: Both wins at 5f but ended last year with narrow defeats over 6f at Yarmouth and Newmarket; off 193 days but she ran very well on reappearance last season; held in one AW start (Tapeta) but she's still a major player.
runners.comment: Ended last year with two narrow defeats; ran very well on her reappearance last season

runners.name: Lady Mariko
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 7f on turf last summer and ended the campaign with a neck defeat at Newmarket (7f, good to soft); unplaced in seven starts on the AW, though, and has never raced over a trip this short.
runners.comment: Unplaced in seven starts on the AW and has never raced over a trip this short

runners.name: Gogo Yubari
runners.spotlight: Busy mare who enjoyed a purple patch around the turn of the year with four wins but she has found it tougher going since; edging back down the weights but would have made more appeal over 5f.
runners.comment: Enjoyed a purple patch around the turn of the year; would have made more appeal over 5f

runners.name: Alpine Girl
runners.spotlight: Last five wins have come on turf but she was successful on Polytrack as a 3yo; ran as though in need of her Newcastle reappearance (6f, Tapeta) in February and no surprise if she fares better this time.
runners.comment: Ran as though in need of her Newcastle reappearance and no surprise if she fares better

runners.name: Fleetwater
runners.spotlight: Ran well when second of seven on her Southwell reappearance (6f, Tapeta) last month and wasn't disgraced when sixth of 11 in a 0-95 contest over C&D nine days ago; considered down in grade back against her own sex.
runners.comment: Wasn't disgraced in a stronger race over C&D nine days ago; considered down in grade
runners.owner: Ms H N Pinniger And Partner

runners.name: Musical Angel
runners.spotlight: Best form for Andrew Balding came on turf, including wins at Brighton (6f, good to firm) and Epsom (7f, good to soft) last year; makes stable debut after 190 days off having changed hands for 50,000gns in October; market informative.
runners.comment: Dual turf winner for Andrew Balding last year; makes stable debut after 190 days off

runners.name: Mercury Day
runners.spotlight: Down to this trip for the first time when beaten two heads into third of seven at Newmarket (good to firm) last June; only 1lb higher but she hasn't been seen since and not sure this sharper track will suit her.
runners.comment: Not seen in 283 days and not sure this sharp 6f will play to her strengths

races.name: Virgin Bet A Good Bet Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div I)

runners.name: Final Night
runners.spotlight: 4-4 over C&D since October and another 4lb rise for his latest success 19 days ago may not preclude another big performance; high on list.
runners.comment: 4-4 over C&D since October and another 4lb rise may not preclude another big performance

runners.name: Champagne Powder
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won a novice and was beaten a head in a handicap in two starts at Chelmsford (1m, Polytrack) around New Year; not so good at Newcastle (1m, Tapeta; first-time cheekpieces) last time but of interest back on Polytrack with blinkers reapplied.
runners.comment: Lesser effort last time but first and second in blinkers on Polytrack before that
runners.owner: S Jones P Burrell & P F I Cole Ltd

runners.name: Helm Rock
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who was beaten less than a length by a subsequent winner at Wolverhampton (8.6f, Tapeta) 13 days ago; races off the same mark but may be vulnerable to younger rivals.
runners.comment: Just beaten by a subsequent winner last time but may be vulnerable to younger rivals

runners.name: Mr Baloo
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who started off last year with a bang, including four wins on Tapeta, but beaten 12 times since despite a sliding mark; others make greater appeal.
runners.comment: Started off last year with a bang but beaten 12 times since despite a sliding mark

runners.name: Valentine Boy
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in a Class 6 over C&D in November but finished over 7l behind Final Night on last month's return in a Class 5; has even more on his plate back in a Class 4.
runners.comment: Over 7l behind Final Night on last month's return and has even more on his plate here

runners.name: Silca Bay
runners.spotlight: All three wins have come here, the latest over this trip in October; finished just over 2l behind Final Night here in December and now 8lb better off but he may just need this first outing since.
runners.comment: All three wins have come here but he may just need this after 110 days off

runners.name: Brave Byreflection
runners.spotlight: Two wins for Alice Haynes include one here (6f); 3lb lower than when a close fifth of 12 over C&D in November and worth a market check on stable debut after four months off.
runners.comment: Two wins for Alice Haynes include one here; worth a market check on stable debut

runners.name: Redbud Sixteen
runners.spotlight: Ran well in the last two of her three starts for Paul Attwater (including on Polytrack) and now makes her handicap debut after 195 days off; market should be revealing; yard also runs Greatbritishdream.
runners.comment: Ran well in last two starts for Paul Attwater; market revealing on handicap debut

runners.name: Greatbritishdream
runners.spotlight: Close fourth of eight in a Lingfield maiden (1m, Polytrack; 200-1) last September but his other two efforts for Paul Attwater either side were modest; makes his handicap debut after six months off; watch market.
runners.comment: Fourth in second of three starts for Paul Attwater; watch market on handicap debut

races.name: Virgin Bet A Good Bet Handicap (London Mile Series Qualifier) (Div II)

runners.name: Candonomore
runners.spotlight: Still 3lb higher than for his easy win at Newcastle (7f, Tapeta) last September but on a fair mark on the pick of his subsequent efforts and the step back up to 1m is no problem; cheekpieces return.
runners.comment: 3lb higher than for his easy win at Newcastle last September but still on a fair mark

runners.name: Zoffandia
runners.spotlight: Very disappointing when last of five behind City Of God in a Chelmsford seller (1m, Polytrack; 4-7) last time but perhaps the run came too soon after gaining second C&D success eight days earlier; a player if bouncing back.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; disappointing last time but the run may have come too soon; a player6/1

runners.name: Berry Clever
runners.spotlight: All three wins have come here, the latest over this trip in October; 6lb higher now but still one for the shortlist if ready to go after 166 days off; Oisin Murphy is an interesting booking, with his record for the stable in past five seasons reading 11233.
runners.comment: All three wins have come here; one for the shortlist if ready to go; Oisin Murphy up

runners.name: Tasdeed
runners.spotlight: Useful form in his early outings but well held in three well-spaced-out starts in handicaps, the latest for this trainer; switch to cheekpieces will need to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Well held in three well-spaced-out starts in handicaps; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Lunario
runners.spotlight: Just 1lb above his last winning mark but 0-9 on the AW and may need the run after six months off.
runners.comment: 0-9 on the AW and may need the run after six months off

runners.name: Broadway Act
runners.spotlight: All five starts on the two Newmarket courses for Charlie Appleby, showing consistent form as a 2yo but virtually pulling up on his 3yo reappearance in 2024; makes his stable/AW debut after two years off having been picked up for 28,000gns in October; market crucial.
runners.comment: Ex-Godolphin; makes his stable/AW debut after two years off; market crucial

runners.name: City Of God
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner for Karl Burke, the latest in a Chelmsford seller (1m, Polytrack; first-time cheekpieces) in February (Zoffandia last of five); not dismissed on stable debut back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner for Karl Burke; not dismissed on stable debut back in a handicap

runners.name: Give Me The Night
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden whose form regressed after showing early promise last year; opposable back from six months off.
runners.comment: 0-6; form regressed after showing early promise last year; opposable after six months off

runners.name: Tennessee Gold
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner (6f/7f) and 3lb below his last winning mark but patchy form since returned to the AW and attempts this far for the first time; hood given a go.
runners.comment: Patchy form since returned to the AW and attempts this far for the first time; hood on

races.name: Virgin Bet Handicap (London Stayers' Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Bashful Boy
runners.spotlight: Veteran who hasn't won since 2023; expensive to follow in recent times but he arrives in form and this is a touch easier again off a career-low mark; can't be left out of calculations.
runners.comment: Arrives in form; this a touch easier again off a career-low mark; enters calculations

runners.name: Mio Amico
runners.spotlight: Best efforts, including two wins this year, have all come for Kyle McHugh; maybe unwise to take his latest Chelmsford victory at face value and he'll need more again if he's to overcome a 7lb rise.
runners.comment: Goes well for Kyle McHugh; will need more again if he's to overcome a 7lb rise

runners.name: Capone
runners.spotlight: Went close over C&D off a much higher mark last spring, but it's been a struggle since and the years look to be catching up; the return here is the main positive.
runners.comment: Been a struggle lately; years look to be catching up; the return here is the main positive
runners.owner: Maximum Racing And D Ffrench Davis

runners.name: Fleurman
runners.spotlight: Not as good now but the handicapper's been quick to ease off and he showed the right attitude to end a losing run over C&D 12 days ago; a 2lb rise seems fair and that won't have hurt his confidence.
runners.comment: Ended a losing run over C&D 12 days ago; a 2lb rise seems fair and he's shortlisted

runners.name: Now The Eagle
runners.spotlight: Twice placed in France but hasn't offered a lot for this yard, suffering with his breathing when tailed off on turf last autumn; hard to recommend.
runners.comment: Twice placed in France but hasn't offered a lot for this yard; hard to recommend

runners.name: Fair Dinkum
runners.spotlight: Dual Lingfield winner who's always had two ways of running; done all his winning after a recent start, which is a concern back from four months off, and he's on a career-high mark after a couple of turf victories last autumn.
runners.comment: Dual Lingfield winner who's always had two ways of running; resumes on a career-high mark

runners.name: Merrijig
runners.spotlight: Won first time out on Polytrack two years ago and made it 2-3 on the AW when closing out last season with a Newcastle victory off 4lb lower; perhaps a bit surprising to see no blinkers tonight but he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Made it 2-3 on the AW when closing out last season with a Tapeta win; one to consider

runners.name: The Truant
runners.spotlight: Well held in two runs for his new yard, one over hurdles, one on Polytrack, late last year; five Irish wins all came after a recent outing and he looks high enough in the weights presently (inexperienced 7lb claimer James Glennon up tonight).
runners.comment: Well held in two runs for his new yard; looks high enough in the weights presently

runners.name: Port Of Entry
runners.spotlight: Five ordinary efforts for Andre Fabre in his younger days (7.5f-1m4f); absent for almost three years since (been gelded) and the market will be the ultimate guide.
runners.comment: Ex-French; absent for almost three years and the market will be the ultimate guide

races.name: Download The Raceday Ready App Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Lessay
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to score over 7f here (10-1; from 5lb higher) in December; form has dipped since February, slowly away, failing to beat a single rival at Newcastle (1m) just over a fortnight ago; needs to rediscover a spark.
runners.comment: Slowly away, failing to beat a single rival at Newcastle (1m) just over a fortnight ago.

runners.name: Coloane
runners.spotlight: 3-36; back to winning ways, clinging on in a similar C&D apprentices' handicap (0-65) on Saturday; the promising Conor Whiteley retains the ride, and she'll be a threat to all if taking her chance again 48 hours on from that success.
runners.comment: 3-36; back to winning ways, clinging on in a similar C&D apprentices' (0-65) on Saturday.

runners.name: King Of Speed
runners.spotlight: Won off this mark over C&D in December, his fourth win at this track; posted a creditable effort (fourth) on penultimate start before failing to get competitive at Doncaster (1m) last week; this looks more suitable.
runners.comment: C&D winner (x4); creditable effort (fourth) on penultimate start; Doncaster is excusable.

runners.name: River Wharfe
runners.spotlight: Came from off the pace (in refitted blinkers) to run down Von Krolock in a 7f apprentices' handicap at Southwell (10-1) on penultimate start; only eighth (King Of Speed fourth) over C&D subsequently; 4lb higher now, with a different claiming rider booked.
runners.comment: Southwell winner (7f) on penultimate start; behind King Of Speed over C&D subsequently.

runners.name: Prince Ali
runners.spotlight: Now 10lb below his last winning mark (here, 9.5f); few positives to take from his efforts this year, beaten in excess of 9l (seventh of ten; 25-1) over 7f here latest; difficult to envisage a revival.
runners.comment: Few positives to take from recent efforts, beaten 9l+ (seventh; 25-1) over 7f here latest.

runners.name: The Ubermensch
runners.spotlight: Slight step forward when never-nearer third in a 7f maiden here (66-1) over five weeks ago; returning to a longer trip should assist on handicap debut; place possible at minimum.
runners.comment: Never-nearer third in a 7f maiden here latest; place possible at minimum on handicap debut

runners.name: Simbas Gift
runners.spotlight: Signs of ability in novice/maiden company but failed to make immediate progress for the switch to handicapping, admittedly with an excuse - suffered interference on the home bend - over C&D last October; easing mark, but best watched off a 168-day absence.
runners.comment: No immediate progress for the switch to handicapping; best watched off a 168-day absence.

races.name: Free Tips Daily On attheraces.com EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Pershaada
runners.spotlight: February foal; 88,000gns yearling; third foal from a 6f 2yo winner (including Listed; RPR 96); closely related to 6f juvenile winner Teej A (87) and a half-sister to 6f AW winner Collusion (74); ideally drawn in stall 1 and trainer Richard Hannon has already struck in the Brocklesby with A Bear Affair.
runners.comment: 88,000gns yearling; third foal from a 6f 2yo winner (inc Listed; 96); ideally drawn in 1

runners.name: Margarets Pearl
runners.spotlight: February foal; second foal from an unraced dam, who was a half-sister to three winners (1m-2m); her sire A'Ali did well with his initial crop in 2025, but this filly is potentially one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Second foal from an unraced dam, who was half-sister to three winners (1m-2m); needs time?

runners.name: Crimson Blaze
runners.spotlight: February foal; 42,000gns yearling; fourth foal from a maiden sprinter (RPR 66); half-sister to the 5f 2yo winner G'Day Mate (91); is bred to be speedy, so it'll be interesting to see how she's positioned in the market.
runners.comment: 42,000gns yearling; fourth foal from a maiden; half-sister to 5f 2yo winner G'Day Mate

runners.name: Fortunate
runners.spotlight: February foal; 12,000gns foal, Â£11,000 yearling; first foal from a 7.4f winner (RPR 76) who was a half-sister to 7f 2yo Listed winner Raydiance; likely to need further eventually, but it's usually prudent to respect a Johnson Houghton juvenile.
runners.comment: 12,000gns foal, Â£11,000 yearling; first foal from a 7.4f winner (RPR 76); let market guide

runners.name: Miss Lizzy
runners.spotlight: March foal; first foal; dam was placed over 1m-1m2f (including AW; RPR 76) and a sister to the 6f 2yo winner Light Angel; the sneaking suspicion is that she might find a few of these other debutantes sharper.
runners.comment: First foal; dam placed over 1m-1m2f (RPR 76) and a sister to the 6f 2yo winner Light Angel

runners.name: Amber Ocean
runners.spotlight: January foal; 70,000euros yearling; fourth foal from an Australian 7.5f-1m winner; half-sister to three winners, the most notable Atlantic Ways (French 7f; RPR 90); Hollie Doyle is booked but she's drawn wide in stall 7.
runners.comment: 70,000euros yearling; fourth foal from an Australian 7.5f-1m winner; Hollie Doyle booked

runners.name: Lover Girl
runners.spotlight: March foal; 42,000gns yearling; second foal from a 5f winner (including AW 2yo; RPR 98), who was a half-sister to two winners, including Glanworth (useful French 6f); noted in the AMO silks, even if the draw (stall 8) could have been kinder.
runners.comment: 42,000gns yearling; second foal from a 5f winner (inc AW; RPR 98); noted in the AMO silks

runners.name: Minnie Bear
runners.spotlight: April foal; 4,000gns yearling; third foal from a 7f-9.4f winner (including 2yo/AW/Group 2; RPR 115); half-sister to the 1m AW winner Fight Your Corner (85); Adam Kirby has already made a positive impression in his fledgling training career.
runners.comment: April foal; 4,000gns yearling; third foal from a 7f-9.4f winner (inc 2yo/AW/Group 2; 115)

races.name: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Caraway
runners.spotlight: Struggled on her comeback run at Southwell (1m; 11-1) but there are reasons to think she'll be better over this trip, not least because her dam was a 1m5f AW Listed winner (RPR 105); interesting to see how she takes to this newly applied hood.
runners.comment: Reasons to think she'll be better over this trip (dam was 1m5f AW Listed winner); hooded.

runners.name: Trust No One
runners.spotlight: Made the breakthrough, off 4lb lower, at Newcastle (1m2f; 6-4 favourite) in January; consistent thereafter (322) without any noticeable uplift for blinkers; returns from a short break in suitable company but with a few of the others open to improvement.
runners.comment: Newcastle winner (1m2f); consistent since without any noticeable uplift for blinkers.

runners.name: Magician Of Riga
runners.spotlight: Gifted the run of the race over C&D (third; 9-1) three weeks ago, but couldn't make it count, weakening behind two better-fancied rivals; a similar scenario could unfold here with stamina reserves inconclusive over 1m4f; 1lb lower.
runners.comment: Weakened behind two better-fancied rivals over C&D three weeks ago; similar scenario?

runners.name: Sunday Sinatra
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; was sporting cheekpieces for the first time when well behind Trust No One at Newcastle (1m2f) on penultimate start and made little impact at Lingfield subsequently; blinkers added over this new trip; risky proposition.
runners.comment: Seven-race maiden; blinkers added over this new trip; lowly rated and a risky proposition.

runners.name: Kelly Burn
runners.spotlight: Better since fitted with a hood, placed over 8.5f here (fillies' maiden) on penultimate start, and ran to a similar level on handicap debut at Doncaster (fifth; 1m2f) latest; pedigree suggests that she'll handle this extra yardage; chance.
runners.comment: Fifth on handicap debut at Doncaster (1m2f); pedigree suggests that she'll handle 1m4f.

runners.name: Forever Perfect
runners.spotlight: Hasn't threatened, making minimal inroads on handicap debut at Southwell (1m; 33-1) over a month ago; now 2lb lower, and set to tackle a new trip with mixed messages on pedigree; surprising if she's the solution to this puzzle.
runners.comment: Minimal inroads on handicap debut at Southwell (1m; 33-1) latest; now 2lb lower; new trip

runners.name: Ballon Rouge
runners.spotlight: Beaten 12l at Chelmsford and Yarmouth but offered a bit more when sixth of nine at Leicester (1m fillies' maiden; 33-1) in October; switches to handicapping off a 181-day absence, and it's possible that this new trip will be within range.
runners.comment: Switches to handicapping off a 181-day absence; possible this new trip is within range.

races.name: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap

runners.name: Elements Of Fire
runners.spotlight: Bought for 32,000gns from the Gosdens; 2-2 since gelded, just doing enough to fend off Epictetus at Chelmsford (7f, Class 4) over a month ago; now 2lb higher as he bids to remain unbeaten for current connections (2-2) and on AW (3-3); solid credentials.
runners.comment: Unbeaten since joining this trainer, just doing enough at Chelmsford (7f); now 2lb higher.

runners.name: One More
runners.spotlight: British form figures 2221321; returned from a slightly less productive stint in Dubai to narrowly edge out Sergeant Pep in a 7f Class 4 at Lingfield (13-8 favourite) ten days ago; with consistency being his forte, he should be one the premises once again from just 2lb higher.
runners.comment: Narrowly edged out Sergeant Pep in 7f Class 4 at Lingfield (13-8 fav) latest; 2lb higher.

runners.name: Kullazain
runners.spotlight: Close to a career best when winning over 6f here (Class 3; 2-1 favourite; from 3lb lower) over Christmas; free and a beaten favourite twice since, so will need to drop the anchor if to make his presence felt over today's longer trip; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Won over 6f here (Class 3) over Christmas; free and a beaten favourite twice since; 7f?

runners.name: Recency Bias
runners.spotlight: Built on a promising start (here and Doncaster) when downing the odds-on favourite in style at Newcastle (7f, novice; 15-8) over a fortnight ago; switches to handicapping on this similar Tapeta surface from a workable opening mark of 83; a fascinating contender.
runners.comment: Downed the odds-on favourite in style at Newcastle (7f, novice) recently; handicap debut.

runners.name: Yankee Dude
runners.spotlight: Won twice as a juvenile, including when justifying favouritism in a Kempton novice (7f) in October 2024; has raced just once since, finishing well down the field on handicap debut at Goodwood (7f) nearly 11 months ago; 3lb lower, and now gelded; monitor only.
runners.comment: 2-3 as juvenile; well beaten on handicap debut at Goodwood (7f) last May; has been gelded.

runners.name: Willem Twee
runners.spotlight: Won the Listed Golden Rose at Lingfield in November 2023 and landed a valuable 6f Class 2 handicap here (off 106) the following summer; has finally slipped back down the weights, giving notice of a return to form (third) in an added visor at Lingfield (7f) 51 days ago; respected.
runners.comment: Gave notice of return to form (third) in added visor at Lingfield (7f) latest; respected.

races.name: Free Race Replays On attheraces.com Handicap

runners.name: Colinski
runners.spotlight: First-time cheekpieces fitted when making winning handicap debut at Lingfield (11.5f, good; for James Owen) last June; mixed form since, although he did finish runner-up at Bath (2m1f, soft) on sole start for Georgina Nicholls; better than position suggests (hampered) on Newcastle AW latest.
runners.comment: Lingfield winner (11.5f, good) for James Owen last June; on 2nd trainer since

runners.name: Kings Cruiser
runners.spotlight: Getting to grips with the task, knuckling down to score narrowly at Chelmsford (about 1m5f) 11 days ago; a subsequent 2lb rise is fair and he has experience of this track to draw upon; one to consider seriously.
runners.comment: Knuckled down to score narrowly at Chelmsford (about 1m5f) recently; player off 2lb higher

runners.name: The Craftymaster
runners.spotlight: Back to winning ways lately, scoring at Chelmsford (1m6f; in blinkers) and Lingfield (2m; in cheekpieces), dictating a steady pace latterly; 2lb higher now but still nicely weighted on historic form so should give another positive showing.
runners.comment: Dictated steady pace when scoring at Lingfield (2m); still nicely weighted off 2lb higher.
runners.owner: R Miles V Miles T Stamp & Partner

runners.name: Regal Glory
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 35 runs came over 7f at Lingfield in December 2022; finished third at Southwell (1m4f) on penultimate start but the percentage call is to oppose.
runners.comment: Sole win from 35 came over 7f at Lingfield in Dec 2022; the percentage call is to oppose.

runners.name: V Power
runners.spotlight: Off the mark over C&D (18-1) in January under Zoe Lewis; disappointing at Newcastle (12.5f) next time and didn't see out his race back here (sixth) last week; tongue-tie now added.
runners.comment: C&D winner (18-1) in January; less effective the last twice; tongue-tie now added.

runners.name: Crystal Guard
runners.spotlight: No AW win since January 2023 (Newcastle, 12.5f); managed to score at Musselburgh (12.5f, good) last July but fortunes dipped markedly towards the end of the turf season; more than enough to prove after layoff.
runners.comment: No AW win since Jan 2023 (Newcastle, 12.5f); more than enough to prove after layoff

runners.name: Alex The Great
runners.spotlight: Won from 14lb higher at Newcastle (2m) over a year ago but he has lost the plot since, beating only a handful of rivals in his last nine starts; no better for cheekpieces at Lingfield (1m4f) latest; stablemate of The Craftymaster.
runners.comment: Has beaten only a handful of rivals in last nine starts; stablemate of The Craftymaster.

runners.name: Shabu Shabu
runners.spotlight: Won a small-field contest at Yarmouth (11.5f) last August; run down late in added blinkers at Pontefract (1m2f) the following month; perfectly entitled to draw benefit from last week's comeback run at Southwell but stamina questionable.
runners.comment: Won a small-field contest at Yarmouth (11.5f) last August; stamina questionable
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xxii & Da Bin Lo

runners.name: Kameel
runners.spotlight: Dual Newcastle scorer (7f maiden/novice) for John Butler, the latest last February; 0-6 for this yard and although close up over 1m2f at Newcastle last month, his stamina is unknown for this far.
runners.comment: 0-6 for J Macey; latest Newcastle third (1m2f) fine; easing mark helpful; stamina unknown

runners.name: Golden Samba
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced; hasn't shown a massive amount and was only a modest third on handicap debut at Bath (1m6f, soft; 50-1) last September; tough to know what to expect on AW return/seasonal reappearance.
runners.comment: Modest third on handicap debut at Bath (1m6f; 50-1) last September; seasonal reappearance.

races.name: Follow @AtTheRaces On X Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Von Krolock
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish gelding whose two course wins in the winter came over 7.2f; continued in form since and is more likely than most at this level to give his running but the drop back in trip doesn't look optimal.
runners.comment: More likely than most at this level to give his running but drop back to 6f isn't optimal

runners.name: Bullington Bry
runners.spotlight: Maiden who was expensive to follow for Jane Chapple-Hyam; proving consistent for this yard and won't mind coming back down in trip, minus the cheekpieces this time, but has had plenty of chances now.
runners.comment: Won't mind coming back down in trip, minus the cheekpieces, but had plenty of chances now

runners.name: Rain Cap
runners.spotlight: Getting on and hard to win with these days; back in form over C&D last time but in a weak-looking 0-55 handicap and that took his record on the AW to 0-17.
runners.comment: Back in form over C&D last time but in a 0-55; that took his record on the AW to 0-17

runners.name: Dancing With Drums
runners.spotlight: Dual winner for Martin Dunne once handicapping late last summer, having been gelded, and remains open to improvement for his new yard; only 2lb higher and market confidence would look significant.
runners.comment: Open to improvement for his new yard; market confidence would look significant

runners.name: South Shore
runners.spotlight: Bit unlucky not to win more than once last year and made a fair comeback at Southwell recently; can fluff the start and/or pull hard, however, and it's easy to argue he's more effective over 5f now.
runners.comment: Fair comeback at Southwell recently; easy to argue he's more effective over 5f now

runners.name: Rye
runners.spotlight: Consistent maiden who's resumed from a short break in good form; decent claims at this level on a few of last autumn's C&D efforts and she's on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Consistent maiden who's resumed from a break in good form; decent claims at this level

runners.name: The Cutest
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden who's struggled in lesser races than this in recent times; would be a shock winner.
runners.comment: Regressive maiden who's struggled in lesser races than this; would be a shock winner

runners.name: Macarone
runners.spotlight: Raced freely, switched from cheekpieces to a first-time visor, and didn't see out 7f last time; a 2lb drop for that sees this dual C&D last autumn back on a workable mark; each-way claims.
runners.comment: 2lb drop sees this dual C&D last autumn back on a workable mark; each-way claims

runners.name: On Key
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden; made most of the running from stall 11 over C&D last time, but that was only a 0-55 and he's positioned wide again.
runners.comment: Made most from stall 11 over C&D last time, but only in a 0-55 and is drawn wide again

races.name: Follow @AtTheRaces On X Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Mythical Isle
runners.spotlight: Maiden who's turned in three solid efforts in C&D handicaps, latterly when hampered late behind East Tyrone a fortnight ago; one to consider in a first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Maiden who's turned in three solid efforts in C&D handicaps; one to consider

runners.name: Forever Noah
runners.spotlight: Better effort back in the (second-time) tongue-tie from stall 9 over C&D last Monday; that was only a 0-55 and his strike-rate is far from exciting but that signalled a return to form.
runners.comment: Better run back in the (second-time) tongue-tie from stall 9 over C&D last Monday; chance

runners.name: Mount Ruapehu
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish gelding who made it 3-4 on Tapeta (found 5f an inadequate test the other occasion) and 2-3 for this yard when winning an apprentice handicap under Jack Nicholls a fortnight ago; only 2lb higher and has every chance of completing the hat-trick.
runners.comment: 3-4 on Tapeta; only 2lb higher and has every chance of completing the hat-trick

runners.name: East Tyrone
runners.spotlight: Odds-on to miss the break and an even shorter price to pull hard; decent record here and looked unlucky for a yard among the winners from the same stall over C&D last time but will always come with risks attached given his run style.
runners.comment: Odds-on to miss the break and an even shorter price to pull hard; capable but risky
runners.owner: Alan And David Fairhurst And P Kirby

runners.name: Simply Blue
runners.spotlight: Plenty of near-misses to go with his only win, which came over 7.2f here off a 7lb higher mark last autumn; latest effort can be ignored but the emphasis will be more on speed, running in a sprint for the first time in a while.
runners.comment: Latest effort can be ignored but the emphasis will be more on speed down from 7f

runners.name: Alyara
runners.spotlight: Not up to much these days; looked a bit unlucky not to finish closer over C&D last time but only in 0-50 classified company and she'd be a surprise winner.
runners.comment: Not up to much these days; would be a surprise winner
runners.owner: Lynn Cullimore Pete Ponting Joe Ponting

runners.name: Dollys Delight
runners.spotlight: 7f turf winner for Julie Camacho last summer; fair debut for her new yard over C&D when last seen six months ago but would have made more appeal over another furlong.
runners.comment: Fair debut for new yard over C&D when last seen; another furlong would have looked ideal
runners.owner: The Good Racing Company Ltd & Partner

runners.name: Gustav Graves
runners.spotlight: Course regular who's going beyond 5f for the first time in almost two years; remains consistent and is on a winning mark but others are preferred over this trip.
runners.comment: Course regular who's going beyond 5f for the first time in almost two years; consistent

runners.name: Rambuso Creek
runners.spotlight: Always had his quirks and again hung left when a beaten-favourite at Southwell last time; usually dropped in and that will likely be the case again from the outside stall, in which case he'll need things to pan out ideally down from 7f.
runners.comment: Usually dropped in and will need things to pan out ideally down from 7f out of stall 9

races.name: Follow @AtTheRaces On Instagram Handicap

runners.name: Ignition
runners.spotlight: 16-race maiden; reasonably competitive in various forms of headgear this year (434) until taking a step back at Lingfield (sixth; 1m4f) latest; drops back noticeably in trip; others are more obvious.
runners.comment: 16-race maiden; reasonably competitive until taking step back at Lingfield (sixth) latest.

runners.name: Book Of Life
runners.spotlight: Competitively handicapped; has finished runner-up three times in 2026, including when touched off in an 8.5f 0-65 here (broke awkwardly) on Saturday; key player over this extra furlong with Ryan Kavanagh back aboard.
runners.comment: Just touched off over 8.5f here (broke awkwardly) on Saturday; player over extra furlong.

runners.name: Three On Thursday
runners.spotlight: 1-18; beaten favourite her last four starts, but is holding form (3523), going under by less than a length to Virtual Hug over 1m4f here (same mark) five weeks ago; fine over this shorter trip, and has a capable claimer back aboard.
runners.comment: 1-18; beaten by less than 1l over 1m4f here (same mark) five weeks ago; shorter trip fine.

runners.name: Kaaranah
runners.spotlight: No win since September 2023 (1m, Kempton; from 9lb higher); caught the eye making late headway (sixth; 16-1) over 8.5f here seven weeks ago; interesting to see if added cheekpieces can instigate a revival; market helpful.
runners.comment: No win since 2023; late headway (sixth) over 8.5f here seven weeks ago; cheekpieces go on.

runners.name: Beachborough Girl
runners.spotlight: Made winning stable debut at Chelmsford (1m2f, Class 6; from 4lb lower) three starts back; forced to settle for minor honours since, including when run down late by He's Our Cracker over 1m2f at Chelmsford; bit more needed on third Wolverhampton visit.
runners.comment: Run down late by He's Our Cracker over 1m2f at Chelmsford latest; bit more needed.

runners.name: Hes Our Cracker
runners.spotlight: Cheekpieces retained and finished off strongly to score at Chelmsford (1m2f; 7-1; with Beachborough Girl third) 25 days ago; 3lb higher, with George Wood taking over in the saddle; obvious chance.
runners.comment: Cheekpieces retained & finished off strongly at Chelmsford (1m2f) latest; now 3lb higher.

runners.name: Its A Love Thing
runners.spotlight: 4lb lower than when winning over C&D last March and 11lb lower than when successful at Pontefract in April; little inspiration from his recent AW efforts, though, beaten 11l, 17.5l, and 23l respectively in three starts in 2026.
runners.comment: 4lb lower than when winning over C&D last March; little inspiration from recent AW efforts

runners.name: Masqool
runners.spotlight: 12-74; sits below last winning mark and filled the runner-up berth at Lingfield (1m2f) on penultimate start; less productive in a change of headgear (visor) over 8.5f here latest; trainer also saddles Three on Thursday.
runners.comment: 12-74; runner-up at Lingfield (1m2f) penultimate start; stablemate of Three On Thursday.

runners.name: Bizarre Law
runners.spotlight: Won just once since June 2022; has beaten just a single rival in his last four starts (either side of wind surgery); a plummeting handicap mark is nowhere near enough to tempt.
runners.comment: Won just once since June 2022; plummeting handicap mark is nowhere near enough to tempt.

runners.name: Gert Lush
runners.spotlight: 5-34, the latest win gained at Sandown (1m1f, good to soft, 2-1 favourite) in May 2023; soundly beaten when last in action at Chepstow (1m2f) in July 2024; she's nine now, so can only be monitored off a substantial absence.
runners.comment: 5-34; soundly beaten when last in action at Chepstow (1m2f) in July 2024; monitor only.
runners.owner: Mrs Muriel Forward & Dr G C Forward

races.name: Supporting Home Run Hounds North West Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Neon Dream
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who, though placed, has yet to match his debut third in a Cheltenham (2m4f, good) maiden hurdle in two subsequent efforts, latest after wind surgery; still sets the standard and the one to beat with the stable going well.
runners.comment: Placed all three runs over hurdles, latest after wind surgery; leading contender.

runners.name: Ballymoreen
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; bought for Â£70,000 after landing a Wexford bumper in October; improved upon her hurdles/stable debut when a respectable third in a 2m mares' contest here in February, shaping as though this step back up in trip would suit; likely to be involved.
runners.comment: Yet to match her Wexford bumper win in two runs over hurdles, but likely to be in the mix.

runners.name: Elusive Pb
runners.spotlight: Placed in two bumpers on heavy ground and confirmed that promise, albeit beaten 17l, when fifth of 14 on hurdles debut at Exeter (2m, good to soft) last month; needs to prove himself on quicker ground, but his pedigree suggests the step up in trip should suit.
runners.comment: Placed in bumpers and confirmed that promise on recent hurdle debut; longer trip may suit.

runners.name: Sea Of Fortune
runners.spotlight: Tongue tied both starts; related to winners but tailed off in a Chepstow bumper on debut and got no further than the first switched to hurdles last month.
runners.comment: Tailed off in his bumper and got no further that the first on last month's hurdle debut.
runners.owner: Mrs I C Sellars And Miss D C Sellars

runners.name: Shantou Premier
runners.spotlight: Â£40,000 buy after finishing fourth in an Irish point nearly a year ago; dam an unraced half-sister to multiple point winner Nightcap Jack; makes hurdle/stable debut returning from an absence.
runners.comment: Displayed promise in an Irish point nearly a year ago; makes hurdle/stable debut.

runners.name: Untapped Potential
runners.spotlight: 47,000euros purchase after winning his third start in Irish points by a wide margin (runner-up and third well held since); half-brother to 2m1f/2m5f hurdle winner Major Barry; looks the type to make an impact over hurdles and one to consider on rules debut.
runners.comment: Irish point winner who looks the type to make an impact over hurdles; one to consider.

runners.name: Lady Blackmore
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in both Irish points last year and, while yet to make a serious impact in three runs over hurdles (all at 2m), she's been steadily progressing; more is needed, but this longer trip could prompt further improvement; one of two for the stable.
runners.comment: Mildly progressive so far over hurdles, but this longer trip could unlock improvement.

runners.name: Ample Warning
runners.spotlight: Showed promise switched to hurdles at Ludlow (2m) in December following a lengthy absence, making the frame, but failed to build on that when a remote seventh of 11 at Warwick (2m, good to soft) last month; needs to leave that effort behind, but remains open to progress; stable runs two.
runners.comment: Needs to leave behind a disappointing run last time, but she remains open to progress.

runners.name: Yellow Warning
runners.spotlight: Bought for Â£10,000 after narrowly winning an Irish point (yielding to soft) in October; 80-1 when unseating at the first in a C&D maiden on last month's stable/hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Irish point winner who was 80-1 when unseating on hurdle debut last month.
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Brian Davies

runners.name: Granite Sands
runners.spotlight: Promising fourth in a Doncaster (2m, good) bumper on debut in March last year; however, he failed to build on that at Southwell (2m, soft) on his return in November, failing to settle and ultimately well held; steps up in trip for this switch to hurdles after another break, with hood added.
runners.comment: Needs to learn to settle, but he could progress if the addition of a hood helps.

runners.name: Georgemani
runners.spotlight: Hinted at ability in his sole start in a point but always behind and pulled up on hurdle/rules debut at Ludlow (2m5f, good to soft) in January; possibly one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Always behind and pulled up on January's hurdle debut; possibly one for longer term.

races.name: Wye Valley Group Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Independent Lady
runners.spotlight: Left behind her qualifying runs when making a winning handicap debut over C&D (good) last month, asserting in good style from a fair yardstick; 5lb rise looks manageable and another bold bid can be expected.
runners.comment: Won on handicap debut over C&D last month; up 5lb; another bold bid can be expected.

runners.name: Jessie Jump Jet
runners.spotlight: Failed to progress for previous stable last season and, having shaped with promise over C&D for new stable in January, took a backwards step at Huntingdon (2m4f, good) last month when finishing last of eight; needs the fitting of cheekpieces to get quickly back on track.
runners.comment: Failed to build on an encouraging stable debut last time; cheekpieces now reached for.

runners.name: Adaay Dancing
runners.spotlight: Not progressing over hurdles and often undermines her chances by failing to settle, but has bits of form that bring her into the reckoning, notably when fourth at Wincanton (1m7f, good to soft) in February; still something to find and stamina to prove over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Has bits of form that bring her into the reckoning but she has stamina to prove.

runners.name: Innisfree Pearl
runners.spotlight: Well held on first two starts over hurdles late last year but, after a respectable fourth back on the Flat (1m6f) in January, made a promising handicap debut over hurdles when keeping on for second at Huntingdon (2m4f, good) last month; 2lb rise looks fair and she is high on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Up 2lb but a leading player after a promising start to handicaps over hurdles last month.

runners.name: Miss Kassiopi
runners.spotlight: 14-race maiden but has returned to something near best on last two starts, finishing runner-up on both occasions, tried in a tongue-tie at Windsor (2m4f, good to soft) in November and without it at Plumpton (near 2m5f, soft) in February after a break; workable mark and should go well again.
runners.comment: 14-race maiden, but in form and likely to go well again off a workable mark.

runners.name: Ballerina Boxer
runners.spotlight: 3m Punchestown handicap hurdle winner off a similar mark in June but below that level since; little encouragement in two starts for new yard, pulling up on soft ground then well beaten (46l) at Stratford; has plenty to prove on current evidence.
runners.comment: Has offered little encouragement in two starts for new stable and easy to look elsewhere.
runners.owner: Mike Harris Racing & Nikki Evans Racing

races.name: Visit Herefordshire Novices' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Bongo Man
runners.spotlight: Initially failed to show any immediate benefit from wind surgery in three subsequent runs (2m to extended 2m4f) but latest effort, fitted with a tongue-tie and going close at Huntingdon (2m, good) last month, was a marked step forward; it will be interesting to see if he can build on that and 3lb claimer is booked.
runners.comment: Needs to back up an improved effort last time, but he's a player if doing so.

runners.name: Graecia
runners.spotlight: Has been disappointing of late and can compromise her chances by over-racing, but would have strong claims if back to the form of her Newbury effort (2m, good to soft) in December, namely second to Cheltenham Festival winner White Noise; worth a second look with promising claimer Daire McConville back in the saddle.
runners.comment: Below best of late but she's hard to ignore given her second to White Noise in December.

runners.name: Getaway With You
runners.spotlight: Won three in a row in November/December (2m3f-2m4f) and scored at Chepstow (2m3f, soft) in February; well-held fourth eight days later at Ffos Las, possibly finding the quick turnaround too much; may resume progress, though the drop in trip is a slight concern.
runners.comment: Latest run may have come too soon latest, but could resume progress after a short break.

runners.name: Granny B
