runners.spotlight: Yet to win in 15 starts over hurdles, having shown some promising placed efforts last summer before her form dipped in the autumn, and she was last seen finishing lame (well held) at Stratford in October; returns on a competitive mark, but with a bit to prove.
runners.comment: 15-race maiden who returns on a competitive mark, but with something to prove.

runners.name: Lord Chamberlain
runners.spotlight: Five-race Flat maiden; clear pick of his three hurdle runs when third of seven at Ludlow (2m, good to soft); needs to leave behind a lesser effort at Huntingdon (2m, good) last time when ridden more prominently, but he brings potential now stepping into handicaps.
runners.comment: Modest effort on latest start but he brings potential switched to handicaps.

runners.name: Desert Halo
runners.spotlight: Hasn't taken to fences this season but came right back to form reverting to hurdles last time, gaining a hard-fought win at Plumpton (2m, good to soft), his first success of any kind; handles most ground and 3lb rise leaves him still well treated on the pick of last season's hurdling form; leading contender.
runners.comment: Well treated if in the same form as for last month's Plumpton win; leading player.

runners.name: Ramaah
runners.spotlight: 1m5f AW Flat winner who has shown only a modest level of ability in a dozen runs over hurdles (2m-2m3f); produced one of his better efforts when third over C&D last month, though beaten 16l; probably place claims at best from out of the weights.
runners.comment: Produced one of his best efforts when third over C&D last month, but needs more again.

runners.name: Bouquet De Paris
runners.spotlight: Shaped with promise in juvenile hurdles in December, and although his run last month on handicap debut was a shade disappointing, the heavy ground likely counted against him; hood worn the last twice is retained, and a better showing can be expected from this 4yo returned to a more suitable surface.
runners.comment: Better can be expected returned to a more suitable surface, on second start in a handicap.

runners.name: For Old Times Sake
runners.spotlight: Has yet to match his Irish point promise in five hurdle races, but did take a step forward on handicap debut in a hood at Huntingdon (2m, heavy) in February before disappointing when a well-backed favourite upped to 2m5f back there the following month (good, hood removed); may do better in time, but difficult to recommend on recent form.
runners.comment: Difficult to recommend on recent form, but given his pointing promise, he may do better.

runners.name: Kingdom Of Stars
runners.spotlight: Has taken time to come to hand over hurdles but his recent third over 2m4f here last month represented by far his best effort, appreciating the better ground; opening mark is no gift, but he remains capable of better in this sphere; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Opening mark is no gift but he remains capable of better in this sphere; interesting.

races.name: Green Dragon Hotel Hereford Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Lelantos
runners.spotlight: 5-27 over hurdles, the latest win gained at Huntingdon (extended 3m1f, good to soft; 2lb higher today) in November; has a shout on that showing but there have been backward steps in his two starts since.
runners.comment: Won at Huntingdon (extended 3m1f) in November; backward steps in his two starts since

runners.name: Malago Rose
runners.spotlight: 19 races for two wins, the latest in January 2025; creditable minor honours on all four starts this season, albeit as favourite on first three occasions; one of those was under Harriet Tucker; stays 3m2f and will probably be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Creditable minor honours all four starts this term, albeit favourite first three occasions

runners.name: Woodythewoodpecker
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden; sent handicapping for last four starts, on last two occasions finishing runner-up on soft going over 3m2f at this track and Fontwell; different ground is forecast today but he's in serious calculations.
runners.comment: 0-10; handicaps on last four starts, on last two occasions runner-up over 3m2f on soft

runners.name: The Long Walk
runners.spotlight: Third at Market Rasen (extended 2m2f, good to soft) on Boxing Day has been the only bright spot in his four races this autumn/winter; now has a 2lb lower mark and goes beyond an extended 2m4f for the first time.
runners.comment: One bright spot in his four races this autumn/winter; first foray beyond an extended 2m4f

runners.name: Despereaux
runners.spotlight: Steadily worked his way back to form this season, culminating in a narrow win over C&D (good to soft; 5lb higher today) in December under 7lb claimer; reverted to a lesser show 19 days ago but perhaps he needed that run after more than three months off.
runners.comment: Narrow C&D win in December; reverted to a lesser show after more than three months off

runners.name: Double Click
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent over the last 12 months or so, but second at Exeter in November showed that he stays 2m7f and his best effort since was on latest outing (a rallying third over 2m4f three weeks ago); much more to prove if the ground is not softer than good, but he has to be considered.
runners.comment: Inconsistent but a better latest effort;  more to prove if ground is not softer than good

runners.name: The Jam Man
runners.spotlight: Veteran whose last win under rules was in 2020 (won a point early in 2024); fourth of 16 at Chepstow in January may look the only positive result in his nine races this season but he was beaten 35l, so that does not persuade; not even with his 37lb fall in the weights this term.
runners.comment: Veteran; has had a 37lb fall in the weights this term but does not persuade

races.name: We Are Farming Minds Charity Partner Mares' Handicap Chase (Challenger Mares' Chase Qualifier) (GBB)

runners.name: Party Vibes
runners.spotlight: Hurdle winner on soft but two chase wins in 2025 were on good to soft, latterly at Cheltenham (about 2m4f) in December, and failures since have come on heavy and soft; needs to jump better than she did at Ludlow in February (over 17l behind Malaita) but she could revive back on good ground.
runners.comment: Both chase wins on good to soft; has struggled on heavy and soft of late; possible reviver

runners.name: Thats Nice
runners.spotlight: Won sole Irish point and two of three novice hurdles (both 2m3f, good to soft); shaky transition to fences but she jumped soundly in a five-runner Newbury race in February before stopping to nothing under pressure; wind surgery since could have helped; retains potential.
runners.comment: Won Irish point; 2-3 in novice hurdles; shaky switch to fences; had wind op since latest

runners.name: Malaita
runners.spotlight: Two chase wins at about 2m4f in early 2024 (soft and good); has not found life easy this winter but 3m found her out on her latest start (first-time tongue-tie; cheekpieces now added) and the return to shorter on good ground should suit; bold bid likely.
runners.comment: Had a difficult winter but return to 2m4f on good ground can suit; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Belle Montrose
runners.spotlight: Has a fondness for Fakenham and she put up a good show when winning her second chase on that track (2m5f, good to soft) in January from out of the weights; has continued in form there since but others might be stronger finishers away from that track.
runners.comment: Remained in form since 2m5f win at Fakenham in January; others might be stronger finishers

races.name: Spa Motors Hereford Handicap Chase

runners.name: Jukebox dEddy
runners.spotlight: Below best on soft two starts ago but he showed tenacity when hanging on to his lead at Huntingdon (3m, good) last month under Rian Corcoran; back up 2lb but he can find further mild improvement; respected.
runners.comment: Three wins from last four chase starts, showing tenacity over 3m latest; respected

runners.name: Walkinthewoods
runners.spotlight: Best at about 2m4f and he's looked to have stamina limitations in the majority of his runs beyond that trip; below his latest winning mark, last May, but he's failed to complete in two of his last three runs since back chasing and some reservations remain.
runners.comment: Failed to complete in 2 of 3 runs since back chasing and best form at about 2m4f

runners.name: Edgewell
runners.spotlight: Won two staying hurdles last February; a little disappointing over fences for this yard, given his  encouraging run at Windsor in November, and he was handed a C&D win (good) recently when his main rival came down late; back up 4lb but well treated on the autumn form.
runners.comment: Two staying hurdle wins in 2025; left clear over C&D recently; well treated on autumn form

runners.name: Braganza Bay
runners.spotlight: Has won two of five handicaps here, hurdles and fences (C&D; good), but he hasn't yet found his form since back from an absence and now tackles fences for the first time since pulling up twice at the end of 2024; below latest winning mark but questions remain.
runners.comment: Pulled up on latest two chase starts in 2024; below best  since absence; well treated now

runners.name: Meranti
runners.spotlight: Showed staying promise over fences earlier in career but he's been in moderate form since back from an absence, pulling up on last two starts.
runners.comment: Has shown staying ability over fences but in moderate form since back from absence

races.name: Richard Layton Charity Evening 16th April Open NH Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: See Me Soon
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-brother to five winners including Go Dante (useful bumper and 2m/2m1f hurdle), Angel Of Harlem (useful 2m4f-3m hurdle/chase) and Little Miss Dante (bumper/2m hurdle); dam bumper/useful 2m-3m hurdle winner for this top stable; very appealing on paper.
runners.comment: Well-bred newcomer from a top stable; very appealing on paper
runners.owner: G Snell And Miss E Snell

runners.name: Crystal Jet
runners.spotlight: Out of a Listed Flat winner; placed twice in the autumn, against others fillies in junior bumpers at Stratford (good to soft) and Huntingdon (good); sports first-time cheekpieces here; ought to be on the premises again.
runners.comment: Out of a Listed Flat winner; placed in two fillies' bumpers in the autumn; respected

runners.name: Final Surprise
runners.spotlight: Placed on Irish point debut last spring (good ground) and also showed clear promise in first two bumpers (one over C&D); considered each-way.
runners.comment: Placed on Irish point debut and has also shown clear promise in both bumpers

runners.name: Falconella
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister to connections' useful bumper/hurdle winner Moon Chime and another hurdle winner; dam 2m hurdle winner, sister to AQPS Flat and smart jumps winner Sweet My Lord; wears a tongue-tie on debut; looks quite interesting.
runners.comment: Half-sister to connections' useful bumper/hurdle winner Moon Chime; quite interesting

runners.name: Plan De Stan
runners.spotlight: Kept on well for clear second of 12 in soft-ground junior bumper over C&D in December and has another big role to play if suited by the faster ground today; had wind op in February.
runners.comment: Clear second of 12 over C&D on debut in December; has another big role to play

runners.name: Georgies Lady
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; half-sister to a French 2m1f hurdle winner; dam unplaced 1m-1m6f (RPR 54), sister to 1m7f Listed Flat and smart jumps winner Noble Prince; hooded on debut, which is somewhat unnerving.
runners.comment: Half-sister to a French hurdle winner; hooded on debut, which is unnerving

runners.name: Cantcallherthat
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam pulled up in points, closely related to winners Carrigmore Mollie (bumper) and Kingston King (chase); stable does well in bumpers; market support will bring her into the equation.
runners.comment: Out of a close relative of a bumper winner; market support will make her of interest

runners.name: Clovers Frontier
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister to connections' winners Mix Of Clover (2m-2m5f hurdle/chase) and Glance From Clover (3m1f chase/3m2f hurdle); dam point winner, half-sister to bumper/useful 2m chase winner Wilde Pastures; well worth a glance in the betting.
runners.comment: Half-sister to stable's winning jumpers Mix Of Clover and Glance From Clover; a possible

runners.name: Cobblers Lad
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-brother to connections' 2m/2m5f hurdle winner Keep On Cobbling; dam 2m6f-3m hurdle/chase winner, sister to smart jumper Rebel Du Maquis; trainer has relatively modest strike-rate in bumpers.
runners.comment: Half-brother to connections' hurdle winner Keep On Cobbling; yard not prolific in bumpers

runners.name: Crystal Anthem
runners.spotlight: 38,000euros 3yo; fourth foal; half-brother to bumper/2m3f hurdle winner Just Golden; dam unraced half-sister to bumper and smart jumps winner O O Seven; stable 0-20 in bumpers in last five seasons; considered only if lively in the betting.
runners.comment: Out of half-sister to smart jumper O O Seven; stable 0-20 in bumpers (last five seasons)

runners.name: Meli Melo dAngos
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam French 2m4f/2m5f chase winner; stable punches above its weight in bumpers; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: First foal from a French chase winner; stable punches above its weight in bumpers

runners.name: Uncle Bob
runners.spotlight: 1,000gns 2yo; first foal; dam unraced sister to Flat winners Doctor Khan Junior (useful 6f/7f) and Margot Robbie (7f), half-sister to useful 6f winner Pocklington; not an obvious type for bumpers based on pedigree.
runners.comment: 1,000gns 2yo; not an obvious type for bumpers based on sprinting pedigree

runners.name: Major Dino
runners.spotlight: Out of a French Flat winner; disappointing joint-favourite when well down the field on debut at Kempton (good; raced too freely) last month but evidently thought capable of better.
runners.comment: Disappointing joint-favourite on debut but evidently thought capable of better

races.name: Join Racing TV Now Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Coolkatie
runners.spotlight: Unexposed mare who was set to be placed at least before last-flight fall in Warwick novice (2m5f, good to soft) in December; safely held on handicap debut at Exeter (2m, heavy) in February, but ought to be better suited by today's trip-and-ground combination; one to consider.
runners.comment: Well held on handicap debut but the ground was heavy; still has potential

runners.name: Livano Bello
runners.spotlight: 0-11 over hurdles and looks exposed, but was placed three consecutive times towards end of 2025 (2m4f-2m5f) and gave another good account when close third of five at Market Rasen (2m2f) three weeks ago; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: 0-11 over hurdles but fairly consistent this season; probably won't be far away

runners.name: Bitsnbuckles
runners.spotlight: Slightly disappointing when only fourth on his latest outing, but posted career-best form when winning readily by over 6l at Southwell (2m4f, good to soft) two starts ago and has an obvious claims if back at that level.
runners.comment: Shade disappointing last time but earlier Southwell win was career-best form

runners.name: Painted Wolf
runners.spotlight: 0-5 over hurdles but left disappointing handicap debut behind when close second of four behind a progressive rival over C&D (good to soft) last month, and his Irish point form suggests he'll be fine on this faster ground.
runners.comment: 0-5 over hurdles but was close second of four behind progressive rival over C&D last month

runners.name: Shotgun Rider
runners.spotlight: Good-ground Irish point winner who was second behind useful rivals on first two hurdling starts (2m4f/2m2f); well beaten after racing too freely on last month's handicap debut but remains open to improvement if he settles better here.
runners.comment: Spoiled chance by racing too freely on h'cap debut; first two hurdle runs were promising

runners.name: Mermaids Cave
runners.spotlight: 7-23 over hurdles, including a good-ground C&D win; beaten favourite in plating company two starts and pulled up in a subsequent handicap, but has slipped to a very good mark and can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Seven hurdle wins; ran poorly last time but has slipped to a very good mark

runners.name: Vicki Vale
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner when trained by Dan Sketon, but absent since her fourth at Ludlow (2m5f, good) for this stable in October 2024 and entitled to benefit from this outing.
runners.comment: Absent since her fourth at Ludlow in October 2024; entitled to need this outing

races.name: Aldiss Juvenile Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Tralee Girl
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat racer who was placed in Listed races at Wetherby (2m, good) and Newbury (2m, soft) on her first two hurdling starts; below that form in another Listed race in January but still very much the one to beat here.
runners.comment: Placed in two Listed hurdles in the autumn and very much the one to beat here

runners.name: Cotton Socks
runners.spotlight: Placed in two junior bumpers towards end of last year (good to soft/soft), but was remote when third on hurdling debut at Market Rasen (2m, good to soft) and needs to improve to trouble an on-song Tralee Girl.
runners.comment: Placed in two junior bumpers; remote when third on recent hurdle debut; more needed

runners.name: Subtle Glance
runners.spotlight: Well down the field in both bumpers and can only be watched on this hurdling debut; hood (worn on debut) refitted.
runners.comment: Well down the field in both bumpers and can only be watched on this hurdling debut

runners.name: Fitzroy Gardens
runners.spotlight: Won on the AW in Ireland in the autumn (1m4f; RPR 76) and bettered inauspicious stable/hurdle debut (wore cheekpieces) when third of eight (albeit well held) at Catterick (1m7f, good; blinkers refitted) last month; can collect another minor prize here.
runners.comment: Flat winner; second hurdle run was much better than her debut; can collect a minor prize

runners.name: Whatcombe
runners.spotlight: Became very disappointing on the Flat for Paul and Oliver Cole and was well beaten on both starts for this stable in the autumn (1m6f/1m4f; wore cheekpieces); has a lot to prove on hurdling debut; no headgear this time.
runners.comment: Became very disappointing on the Flat and has a lot to prove on hurdling debut

runners.name: Winstons Oath
runners.spotlight: Showed only minor promise in his three bumpers and was behind Cotton Socks when well-beaten sixth on recent hurdling debut at Market Rasen.
runners.comment: Showed only minor promise in three bumpers and was well beaten on hurdling debut

races.name: Aldiss "Chasing Excellence" Novices' Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Kilaro
runners.spotlight: Won good-ground maiden point in November and followed up in good style in Musselburgh bumper (good to soft) on rules debut in December; not discredited when third in quite valuable Kelso bumper (good to soft; hooded) last month and Tim Easterby is evidently very keen to launch his chasing career.
runners.comment: Point/bumper winner; makes chasing debut having bypassed hurdles; interesting

runners.name: Walking On Air
runners.spotlight: Very useful on his good days, the latest when third in Great Yorkshire Chase at Doncaster (3m, soft; first-time cheekpieces) in January; subsequent 3m6f Cheltenham Festival defeat can be excused and he'll be fine on the forecast good ground here, but he's 0-10 over fences and very inconsistent.
runners.comment: Very useful on his good days but 0-10 over fences and very inconsistent

runners.name: Sonigino
runners.spotlight: Very useful hurdler with 6-22 strike-rate; has shown his form on only one of three starts since returning from a long absence this season, when close third at Sandown (2m4f, good) two starts ago, but has obvious claims if on song for this chasing debut.
runners.comment: Very useful hurdler; showed form on only one of three starts this season; chase debut
runners.owner: Sir A Ferguson G Mason L Hales The Late J Hales

races.name: Cecil & Sheila Buttifant Memorial Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Annie Nail
runners.spotlight: Won a chase here (2m5f, good) last May and returned to form with 66-1 second in soft-ground C&D hurdle in December; safely held twice since and too inconsistent to rely upon, but still features prominently in calculations.
runners.comment: Far too inconsistent to rely upon but runs this track well and enters calculations

runners.name: Best Women
runners.spotlight: Dual Flat and dual hurdle winner when based in France but didn't achieve much when third in 1m7f novice hurdle on British debut in November (first run after long absence) and was pulled up in a subsequent handicap (1m7f); has a lot to prove on first venture beyond 2m6f; had wind op in February.
runners.comment: Ex-French 7yo; has not shown enough on her two British starts this season

runners.name: Four Decades
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in latest qualifying race (raced too freely) but her midfield finish at Kempton (2m, good to soft) two starts ago was promising, and her pedigree suggests she'll be suited by this hike up in trip on her handicap debut.
runners.comment: Showed promise in 2m Kempton maiden in February and is bred for this much longer trip

runners.name: Abbey Law
runners.spotlight: Won 2m maiden hurdle here (good) last April but both handicap runs over that trip this year have been very disappointing; getting back on good ground ought to aid her cause, but she failed to convince stamina-wise when tried over 2m5f last spring and has even further to go today; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: 2m maiden winner here last April but well beaten in both handicaps this year; new trip

runners.name: Callmesusie
runners.spotlight: Maiden point winner in 2024; showed no significant promise in maiden hurdles this season (2m-2m4f; refused to race on one occasion) and is 14lb out of the weights for this handicap debut; needs combination of longer trip and new tongue-tie to enable significant improvement.
runners.comment: Winning pointer; no promise over hurdles so far; 14lb wrong for this handicap debut

races.name: Watch On Racing TV Handicap Chase

runners.name: Brother Boris
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who bids to complete a Fakenham hat-trick after two wide-margin wins in two-finisher contests this year (3m5f, soft/3m, good to soft); the value of his form is tricky to evaluate and today's faster ground may not be ideal but, nevertheless, another bold bid seems likely.
runners.comment: Bids for course hat-trick after two wide-margin wins in two-finisher events
runners.owner: Elb Bloodstock Ltd And R L Banks

runners.name: Cloudy Wednesday
runners.spotlight: Three-time course winner, including last two editions of this race (both on good ground); returned from layoff with two pretty good efforts at Huntingdon (2m7f/2m4f) last month and commands respect back here.
runners.comment: Won the last two editions of this race and returns here after two good runs last month

runners.name: Another Mystery
runners.spotlight: Runner-up over hurdles here (2m4f, good; first-time blinkers) on latest rules start last June and competes off a 10lb lower mark today, but his chasing strike-rate is 0-11 and this year's point form is uninspiring.
runners.comment: Second over hurdles here last spring but this year's point form is uninspiring

runners.name: Reforme
runners.spotlight: Probably fortunate to win off today's mark over C&D (good to soft; leader fell at final fence) in October, but that was still a good effort and his next two runs were respectable; contender if tuned up after 97-day break.
runners.comment: Shade fortunate to win over C&D in October but still considered

races.name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Mares' Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: LAmandine
runners.spotlight: Out of a French hurdle/chase winner; represents stable with excellent bumper strike-rate (25%) and kept on for third of 11 on debut at Ludlow (good to soft) in January; major player.
runners.comment: Third of 11 on debut at Ludlow in January; stable has excellent bumper record

runners.name: Hidden Fortune
runners.spotlight: Stoutly bred mare who joined Dan Skelton after winning by 5l on Irish point debut (good to yielding) last spring; second of five in race that developed into a sprint at Market Rasen (good to soft) on rules debut in November; that form was boosted when the winner overcame a penalty last week, and this mare can probably improve if there's more emphasis on stamina here.
runners.comment: Irish point winner; second on rules debut in November (form recently boosted by winner)

runners.name: Crescendo Lady
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; sister to low-grade hurdle winner Libor Lad; dam well beaten in bumpers; makes only limited appeal on paper.
runners.comment: Fifth foal; sister to low-grade hurdle winner Libor Lad; limited appeal on paper

runners.name: On The Cobbles
runners.spotlight: First foal from unraced sister to useful jumper Golden Taipan; stable 0-42 in bumpers during the last five seasons.
runners.comment: First foal from unraced sister to useful jumper Golden Taipan; yard very quiet in bumpers

runners.name: Blues Secret Gem
runners.spotlight: With a good stable and made quite encouraging debut when fourth of seven at Plumpton (good to soft) last month; no surprise if she makes the necessary progress here.
runners.comment: With a good stable and made quite encouraging debut when fourth of seven at Plumpton

races.name: Lugard Handicap (Class 4) (Course B+2) (Turf)

runners.name: Almighty Lightning
runners.spotlight: Beat a subsequent winner by 2l on his official debut over C&D and looks well handicapped on that evidence, especially as there should be more to come; shaped well when third in a recent trial, again under Zac Purton.
runners.comment: Beat a subsequent winner by 2l on his official debut over C&D and looks well handicapped

runners.name: Conspirator
runners.spotlight: Placed the once but he's now 0-13 in Hong Kong and well down the pecking order here.
runners.comment: Placed the once but he's now 0-13 in Hong Kong and well down the pecking order here
runners.owner: Ng Sun Wah Au Yau Lin & Alan Leung Wai Zen

runners.name: Enjoy Golf
runners.spotlight: Poor strike-rate and inconsistency has crept into his game; further probably suits better as well.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate and inconsistency has crept into his game; further probably suits

runners.name: Star Performer
runners.spotlight: A winner in South Africa but unplaced in 12 starts in Hong Kong; has shown ability in defeat but hard to warm to here from stall 1.
runners.comment: A winner in South Africa but unplaced in 12 starts in Hong Kong

runners.name: Zouper Fellow
runners.spotlight: Clearly on a tough mark to begin with but he's beginning to show more and is down another 2lb for latest 3l defeat; that was over 6f and he's quick enough for 5f; not ruled out with Andrea Atzeni taking over.
runners.comment: Clearly on a tough mark to begin with but he's beginning to show more
runners.owner: Dr Philip Ho Kin Hoi Jason Ho Chung Man Et Al

runners.name: Falcon Hunter
runners.spotlight: Reserve; huge odds when an eyecatching third over 6f at Happy Valley where plenty went wrong; could be on the upgrade but this will be his first run at 5f.
runners.comment: Reserve; huge odds when an eyecatching third over 6f at Happy Valley

runners.name: Super Sixty
runners.spotlight: A beaten favourite in his last three official races, all over 5f at Happy Valley; hasn't been running badly though and he won a recent trial; two fairly heavy defeats on his previous visits here are slightly worrying.
runners.comment: Proving expensive to follow but continues to run well and he won a recent trial

runners.name: Invictus
runners.spotlight: Two wins and a second to show from five runs in trials and could be interesting on this official debut, especially should the betting bode well.
runners.comment: Two wins and a second to show from five runs in trials and could be interesting

runners.name: Casa Buddy
runners.spotlight: Makes official Hong Kong debut but has plenty of experience in trials, though unfortunately in defeat; could be kicking off on a tough mark.
runners.comment: Has plenty of experience in trials, though unfortunately in defeat

runners.name: Mega Captain
runners.spotlight: Another official debutant who has displayed plenty of ability in trials, with figures of 121.
runners.comment: Another official debutant who has shown plenty of ability in trials, with figures of 121
runners.owner: Leung Tin Fu Leung Kit Faat & Ma Hing Man

runners.name: Graceful Heart
runners.spotlight: Makes official debut after finishing 9212 in his trials so he clearly has plenty of ability; hard to dismiss.
runners.comment: Makes official debut after finishing 9212 in trials so he clearly has plenty of ability

runners.name: Parrot Blessing
runners.spotlight: No better than sixth in as many trials and wouldn't be the obvious answer.
runners.comment: No better than sixth in as many trials and wouldn't be the obvious answer

runners.name: Sea Diamond
runners.spotlight: First official run was better than the second, both here over 6f; since beaten in a trial; others preferred.
runners.comment: First official run was better than the second, both here over 6f; since beaten in a trial

runners.name: Precision Mind
runners.spotlight: Has won two of his three trials, the latest under Karis Teetan when well on top in the finish; looks promising.
runners.comment: Has won two of three trials, the latest under Karis Teetan when well on top in the finish
runners.owner: Dr & Mrs Allan Chau Kit Ho And Mr & Mrs Chau Man Kwok

runners.name: Rapid Phantom
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in two his five trials but others would have shown more in that scene.
runners.comment: Runner-up in two his five trials but others would have shown more in that scene
runners.owner: Karson Choi Ka Tsan & Donnie Yen Chi Tan

runners.name: Oneshot
runners.spotlight: Reserve; had placed form but unable to win any of his seven trials; best watched.
runners.comment: Reserve; had placed form but unable to win any of his seven trials; best watched
runners.owner: Anthony Chan Wai Choi Wong Tsz Wa & Kwok Ying Lung

races.name: Severn Handicap (Class 5) (Course B+2) (Turf)

runners.name: Majestic Delight
runners.spotlight: Well drawn over C&D 22 days ago - as is again the case - and he did that comfortably off 6lb lower; should run well again with Hugh Bowman maintaining the partnership.
runners.comment: Well drawn over C&D 22 days ago - as is again the case - and he did that comfortably

runners.name: Supreme Winner
runners.spotlight: 0-18 and has dropped to a basement mark; second in a recent trial but remains opposable.
runners.comment: 0-18 and has dropped to a basement mark; second in a recent trial but remains opposable

runners.name: Oriental Surprise
runners.spotlight: Nearly shed his maiden status when edged out by Lucky Generations at Happy Valley in February but only fifth back there a month ago when there were no clear excuses; Zac Purton is back on but from stall 14.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden; Zac Purton is back on but from stall 14

runners.name: Smart Engineer
runners.spotlight: No places in 12 starts; did go clear over 7f here last time before weakening late on so the drop in trip is understandable.
runners.comment: Clear over 7f here latest before weakening late on so the drop in trip is understandable

runners.name: Noble Fans
runners.spotlight: Hadn't been pulling up in any trees in official races before winning a recent trial; others look safer.
runners.comment: Hadn't been pulling up in any trees in official races before winning a recent trial

runners.name: King Alloy
runners.spotlight: Close third behind Lucky Generations and Oriental Surprise over 6f at Happy Valley two runs back when attempting to make all from a wide draw; had another tough draw back at 5f last time so that was another good run; better boxed here so considered.
runners.comment: Has been running well from tough draws and better boxed here so considered
runners.owner: Lun Ka Leung & Yeung Hiu Yu

runners.name: Shine Bright
runners.spotlight: Weakened from the front over 7f here last week but he's had plenty of chances over this far.
runners.comment: Weakened from the front over 7f last week but he's had plenty of chances over this far

runners.name: Circuit Duffy
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent but better drawn than last time and did go close over C&D in January; can win again off this sort of mark.
runners.comment: Inconsistent but better drawn than last time and did go close over C&D in January
runners.owner: Cheung Ka Shing & Jason Cheung Cho Yee

runners.name: Iconical
runners.spotlight: Downgraded from Class 4s and well drawn for one who likes to race prominently; doesn't find winning easy but has each-way claims.
runners.comment: Downgraded from Class 4s and well drawn for one who likes to race prominently

runners.name: Cirrus Speed
runners.spotlight: Tends to get going too late but it was a Class 4 in which he was a closing sixth here 22 days ago; would have been tempting but for his wide draw.
runners.comment: It was a Class 4 in which he was a closing sixth here 22 days ago; draw is a negative

runners.name: Lucky Generations
runners.spotlight: 6lb higher than for his short-head defeat of Oriential Surprise at Happy Valley (6f) and his profile is very up an down; finished last in a recent trial.
runners.comment: 6lb higher than for his short-head win at Happy Valley; not sure to repeat that form

runners.name: Robot Knight
runners.spotlight: Only 1-25 overall and no better than fifth in five starts since moving yards; unconvincing.
runners.comment: Only 1-25 overall and no better than fifth in five starts since moving yards; unconvincing

runners.name: Concorde Star
runners.spotlight: Reserve; drawn wide last time and had found some form prior to that; perhaps better at Happy Valley, though.
runners.comment: Reserve; drawn wide last time and had found some form prior to that
runners.owner: Philip Yu Chi Wai & Dr Jennie Peterson Yu

runners.name: Always Fluke
runners.spotlight: Sole win in 19 starts came over C&D in September 2023; not disgraced in a higher grade the last twice and the form of his near miss in a Class 5 over C&D in February has worked out well; considered.
runners.comment: Runner-up here the last time he contested a Class 5 and that form has worked out well

runners.name: View All Things
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in all ten starts, the last three on dirt; off 158 days but did win a recent barrier trial; however, others are more solid.
runners.comment: Did win a recent barrier trial but on official form others are more solid

races.name: Pollock Handicap (Class 4) (Course B+2) (Turf)

runners.name: Fighting Machine
runners.spotlight: Beaten under 3l here last time when never dangerous from off the pace; losing run is mounting up.
runners.comment: Beaten under 3l last time when never dangerous from off the pace; losing run mounting up

runners.name: Smart Fat Cat
runners.spotlight: 0-10 but has been very competitive on occasions and didn't really get the breaks back at 7f most recently; eyeshield replaces cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Competitive on occasions and didn't really get the breaks back at 7f most recently
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Admund Au Chi Hoe

runners.name: Smiling Falcon
runners.spotlight: Had Victor Supreme behind when a closing third over C&D in February but couldn't land a blow when behind that rival back here a month ago; if forgiven that then he's clearly a contender.
runners.comment: Below par here last time but has definite claims on his close third the run before

runners.name: Big Return
runners.spotlight: This maiden hasn't been running at all badly but continues to be found wanting when it matters; has questions to answer at 1m and he's drawn wide.
runners.comment: Not running badly but has questions to answer at 1m and he's drawn wide

runners.name: Flying Amani
runners.spotlight: Yet again he was slowly away over C&D a month ago so did well to get so close in the first-time cheekpieces, finishing ahead of some of today's rivals; however, that tendency to miss the kick remains a concern.
runners.comment: Big run to be fourth here last time considering he again missed the break

runners.name: Derrynblue
runners.spotlight: Hasn't had many chances but has been finishing well held and is best watched for now.
runners.comment: Hasn't had many chances but has been finishing well held and is best watched for now
runners.owner: Kenneth Kong Siu Chee & Elaine Kong Yick Ning

runners.name: Amazing Gaze
runners.spotlight: Has yet to win and his best runs have come under Zac Purton who looks elsewhere today; probably placed at best from stall 11.
runners.comment: Has yet to win and his best runs have come under Zac Purton who looks elsewhere today

runners.name: Lucky Year
runners.spotlight: Very unexposed and was a close sixth over C&D last time despite hitting traffic, finishing upside Victor Supreme; considered.
runners.comment: Very unexposed and was a close sixth over C&D last time despite hitting traffic

runners.name: Multisuperstar
runners.spotlight: Only raced four times and best effort yet when fourth of 14 over C&D two weeks ago; never nearer than at the finish from a midfield position; that's something to build on.
runners.comment: Only raced four times and best effort yet when fourth of 14 over C&D two weeks ago

runners.name: Super Sicario
runners.spotlight: Both wins have come at Happy Valley (1m/1m1f) off lower marks in a lower grade; poorly drawn.
runners.comment: Both wins have come at Happy Valley (1m/1m1f) off lower marks in lower grade; poorly drawn
runners.owner: Tony Lau Yiu Tong & Thomas Lau Sum Fai

runners.name: Victor Supreme
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced; has got a bit far back over 1m here the last twice but was running on well; not drawn great but Zac Purton takes over; has to be considered.
runners.comment: Lightly raced; has got a bit far back over 1m here the last twice but was running on well

runners.name: Kyrus Treasure
runners.spotlight: Has hinted at winning potential in five official races but was down the field in recent trials.
runners.comment: Has hinted at winning potential in five official races but down the field in recent trials

runners.name: Mr Cool
runners.spotlight: 7f winner here a year ago; 0-10 in the meantime but hard to fault his recent efforts here at 6f/7f, prior to winning a trial only last week; has shown that he stays this far; player.
runners.comment: Hard to fault his recent efforts here at 6f/7f; has shown that he stays this far

races.name: Plunkett's Handicap (Class 4) (Course B+2) (Turf)

runners.name: California Bay
runners.spotlight: 0-16 but deserves a break as he was twice bang there in C&D races under Zac Purton before stall 14 found him out three weeks ago; Purton wasn't aboard that day but he's back on now from stall 6; big shout.
runners.comment: Unfortunate to retain his maiden status and was drawn in 14 when fifth last time

runners.name: Illuminous
runners.spotlight: 0-19 since a winning debut in Hong Kong more than two years ago; however, was beaten only 4l on last month's return from a break (6f; stays 1m+) and plenty went wrong that day; lurks on a dangerous mark.
runners.comment: Has got himself well handicapped and positives to take from last month's run here
runners.owner: Christina Y C Ting Chen Wen Bin Et Al

runners.name: Lucky Man
runners.spotlight: C&D win last May his sole success in 20 starts; signed off for last yard on a downer.
runners.comment: C&D win last May his sole success in 20 starts; signed off for last yard on a downer

runners.name: Flying Knight
runners.spotlight: Not without hope on his penultimate effort here (7f) as he was hot on the heels of the principals; has disappointed since, however.
runners.comment: Not without hope on penultimate effort here as he was hot on the heels of the principals
runners.owner: Shi Ke Min & Frankie Fang Yuan

runners.name: Duke Of Orange
runners.spotlight: Held up but was closing on the principals when fourth over 6f here a month ago; that was only his fourth official run and he retains potential, especially now upped to 7f; stall 14 clearly not ideal, however.
runners.comment: Promising last run and brings potential now raised to 7f; stall 14 not ideal, however

runners.name: Gor Gor
runners.spotlight: His narrow win over 6f here in February is his standout piece of form and he was only midfield off this 6lb higher mark a month ago; hasn't shone over 7f previously.
runners.comment: His narrow win over 6f here in February is his standout piece of form; beaten since

runners.name: I Excelle
runners.spotlight: Some positives to take from his 6f/7f runs here and he might be improving having since won a trial; couldn't dismiss from a useful draw.
runners.comment: Positives to take from his 6f/7f runs and has since won a trial; probaby improving

runners.name: Lets Have Fun
runners.spotlight: Australian form wasn't bad but he hasn't got anything going at all in Hong Kong.
runners.comment: Australian form wasn't bad but he hasn't got anything going at all in Hong Kong

runners.name: Quick Money
runners.spotlight: Won a 5f novice here in May of last year but he's been none too threatening in handicaps; unraced beyond 6f.
runners.comment: Won a 5f novice here in May of last year but he's been none too threatening in handicaps

runners.name: King Cannon
runners.spotlight: Only eighth over 6f at Happy Valley on official debut and underwhelming in trials, before and since that run.
runners.comment: Only eighth over 6f at Happy Valley on official debut and underwhelming in trials

runners.name: Gallant Epoch
runners.spotlight: Some good runs have triggered this career-high mark off which he made the frame from a wider stall here last time; that was in February but he recently confirmed his wellbeing in winning a trial; high up the list with Nichola Yuen claiming 10lb.
runners.comment: Running well here before taking a recent trial; respected with Nichola Yuen claiming 10lb
runners.owner: Dr & Mrs Simon Suen Siu Man And Chloe Suen

runners.name: Robot Star
runners.spotlight: Made it third-time lucky over 7f here in January and has continued to run well at 1m, looking unlucky last time; Zac Purton jumps off to partner California Bay but this 4yo appeals as a likely contender.
runners.comment: Made it third-time lucky over 7f here in January and has continued to run well at 1m

runners.name: Cleansweeper
runners.spotlight: Makes official debut after a number of uninspiring efforts in trials.
runners.comment: Makes official debut after a number of uninspiring efforts in trials
runners.owner: Tai Woon Shing & Chan Koon Man

runners.name: Lucky Ranger
runners.spotlight: Evens favourite for both his races despite wide draws; finished second over 6f and then comfortably went one better over this far; has since continued the good work in a trial; raised 7lb for last time but has the potential to rate higher.
runners.comment: Up 7lb for last time but it's been a good start and he has the potential to rate higher

races.name: Peel Handicap (Class 4) (Course B+2) (Turf)

runners.name: Come Fast Fay Fay
runners.spotlight: Prominent in the betting when a half-length winner of a Class 5 here in February and finished well to take second behind Mr Incredible in a Class 4 three weeks ago; a 6lb pull on the winner could make it interesting.
runners.comment: Second to Mr Incredible here and a 6lb pull on the winner could make it interesting

runners.name: Good Fortune
runners.spotlight: Reserve; last month's fifths here were close ones and there are races in him off this mark, especially if ridden a bit handier.
runners.comment: Reserve; last month's fifths here were close ones and there are races in him off this mark

runners.name: Riding High
runners.spotlight: Made all in a Class 5 here on New Year's Day and has continued to run well at this level; well drawn to attack so feared.
runners.comment: Made all in a Class 5 here on New Year's Day and has continued to run well at this level

runners.name: Sunny Q
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in October and only nosed out by Goldentronicmighty the next time; chance on all that form but has dropped his guard of late.
runners.comment: On his October runs here he has a chance but has just dropped his guard of late

runners.name: Everstar
runners.spotlight: Dual winner at Happy Valley but ran moderately there before another heavy defeat here two weeks ago; others safer.
runners.comment: Best form at Happy Valley and yet ran poorly there before a heavy defeat here latest
runners.owner: Jackie F C Hang Colin L P Cheung & Fredrick F C Lun

runners.name: Little Monster
runners.spotlight: Twice runner-up here (5f/6f) but needs to up his game after two lesser efforts and was only fourth in a recent trial.
runners.comment: Needs to up his game after two lesser efforts and was only fourth in a recent trial

runners.name: Goldentronicmighty
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner; on a fair mark and didn't get the best of runs when beaten 3l on his return to Class 4 level here two weeks ago; possibilities.
runners.comment: Didn't get the best of runs when beaten 3l on his return to Class 4 level here recently

runners.name: Mr Incredible
runners.spotlight: Trial winner who was only 4-1 for official debut over C&D three weeks ago and made rapid headway from off the pace to score cosily from Come Fast Fay Fay; hit with a 7lb rise but he's dangerously unexposed.
runners.comment: Trial winner who won cosily over C&D after making rapid headway from off the pace

runners.name: Thousand Spirit
runners.spotlight: Second from stall 13 over C&D two runs back and his subsequent defeat was at Happy Valley; might be better suited to this track and that penultimate effort makes him a serious player.
runners.comment: Second from stall 13 over C&D two runs back and his subsequent defeat was at Happy Valley

runners.name: Money Tycoon
runners.spotlight: No real positives to take from his four runs here and again offered little in a recent trial.
runners.comment: No real positives to take from four runs here and again offered little in a recent trial

runners.name: Call Me Success
runners.spotlight: Has been finishing his races off nicely over 6f and again in winning a recent trial; gives the impression that 7f will suit in time but he's looking progressive.
runners.comment: Has been finishing his races off nicely over 6f and again in winning a recent trial

runners.name: Dragon Hall
runners.spotlight: Had plenty of chances in trials and only sixth and ninth in two runs here over 5f; unconvincing.
runners.comment: Had plenty of chances in trials and only sixth and ninth in two runs here over 5f

runners.name: Healthy Pony
runners.spotlight: Modest form in trials before finishing last on his offical debut (C&D) and he's since suffered the same fate in another trial.
runners.comment: Finished last on official debut and form in trials is no more encouraging

runners.name: Supreme Voyager
runners.spotlight: Reserve; raised his profile with a big run from stall 12 here last time and he's lightly raced; could feature if getting a run.
runners.comment: Reserve; raised his profile with a big run from stall 12 here last time
runners.owner: 24/25 Dennis C H Yip Trainer Syndicate (2)

runners.name: Lucrative Eight
runners.spotlight: A bit of a mixed bag in his trials but he did win once and should have a future on the track; whether this initial mark is at all generous remains to be seen.
runners.comment: A bit of a mixed bag in his trials but he did win once and should have a future

runners.name: Superb Spirit
runners.spotlight: Form figures of 1113 in his trials and it was a nice run under Zac Purton in the defeat; promising.
runners.comment: Form figures of 1113 in his trials and it was a nice run under Purton in the defeat

races.name: Bluff Handicap (Class 3) (Course B+2) (Turf)

runners.name: Looking Bright
runners.spotlight: 0-6 in Hong Kong but, with tongue-tie added, finished close up over C&D on last two appearances; obvious contender if in similar form again.
runners.comment: 0-6 in Hong Kong but has finished close up over C&D on last two appearances

runners.name: Fast Responder
runners.spotlight: Yet to win this season but scores high marks for his consistency over C&D, and benefits from Nichola Yuen's very useful 10lb claim today; good chance.
runners.comment: Very consistent over C&D this season and has good 10lb claimer enlisted today
runners.owner: Hong Kong St John Presidents Club Syndicate

runners.name: Kansas
runners.spotlight: Smart and speedy 2yo when based in Ireland, albeit winning only once from nine starts; makes Hong Kong debut after six-month break and is drawn on the wing but still looks interesting.
runners.comment: Smart and speedy 2yo when based in Ireland; makes Hong Kong debut today

runners.name: Wonderstar
runners.spotlight: Well below best over 6f in recent weeks but will be suited by this drop back in trip and is now 3lb lower than when winning over C&D in October; can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Well below best over 6f lately but won off 3lb higher over C&D in October

runners.name: Whizz Kid
runners.spotlight: Smart 5f performer in his prime but has regressed; made the frame on only one of his eight starts this season; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Smart 5f performer in his prime but has regressed; look elsewhere

runners.name: Real Time
runners.spotlight: Won over 7f when based in New Zealand but was well beaten over that trip on recent Hong Kong debut.
runners.comment: Won over 7f when based in NZ but was beaten over that trip on recent Hong Kong debut.

runners.name: Alpha Strike
runners.spotlight: Made all over C&D on Hong Kong debut in October and put two disappointing runs behind him when fourth behind Glowing Praises over C&D in February; fitted with a hood today; can make presence felt again.
runners.comment: Made all over C&D on HK debut in October and was back on track on latest outing

runners.name: Tactical Ace
runners.spotlight: 2-5 in Australia but made low-key Hong Kong debut over C&D three weeks ago and needs to have taken significant benefit from that outing.
runners.comment: 2-5 in Australia but made a low-key Hong Kong debut over C&D last month

runners.name: Lucky Candy
runners.spotlight: Well down the field on first three Hong Kong starts (6f-1m) but his strong-finishing sixth over C&D last month was much better, and he now has something to build on.
runners.comment: Well down the field on first three HK starts but recent C&D sixth was much better
runners.owner: Yeung Wai Kwong & Lee Sheung Ping

runners.name: Blue Illusion
runners.spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo; ran on strongly to cause 97-1 shock at Happy Valley (5f) in December, his third Hong Kong start, but three lesser efforts have followed and he now has questions to answer.
runners.comment: Shock winner at Happy Valley in December but three lesser efforts have followed

runners.name: Glowing Praises
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D on Hong Kong debut in February and probably bettered that performance when very close third over C&D last month; nicely drawn towards the nearside rail today and Zac Purton takes the ride; respected.
runners.comment: C&D winner on Hong Kong debut in February; placed since; Zac Purton booked; respected

runners.name: Tactical Horizons
runners.spotlight: Made winning debut over this trip in Australian maiden last June; not seen since but market support will bring him into the equation.
runners.comment: Won 5f maiden in Australia on debut last June; this is his first run since
runners.owner: Elvis Kwok Kam Chuen Winkle Lee Wing Ho Et Al

races.name: Sprint Cup (Group 2) (Course B+2) (Turf)

runners.name: Ka Ying Rising
runners.spotlight: Has completely dominated the Hong Kong sprinting scene over the last two seasons, with current winning sequence of 18 containing eight Group 1s (one in Australia); drops back in trip after easily beating Helios Express over 7f six weeks ago; impossible to oppose, as usual.
runners.comment: Has completely dominated the Hong Kong sprinting scene over the last two seasons

runners.name: Helios Express
runners.spotlight: Placed several times behind Ka Ying Rising since start of last season, including when second in this race last year and in 7f Group 1 last month; ought to give another good account and collect another minor prize.
runners.comment: Placed several times behind Ka Ying Rising; should collect another minor prize

runners.name: Raging Blizzard
runners.spotlight: Belied 112-1 odds when staying on to snatch second (no threat to easy winner Ka Ying Rising) in Hong Kong Sprint in December and again made the frame at triple-digit odds in C&D Group 1 in January; below that form over 7f last time but can contest the minor honours on return to sprinting.
runners.comment: Below form over 7f last time but earlier 6f form puts him in contention for a place

runners.name: Beauty Waves
runners.spotlight: Did well to win Happy Valley handicap from wide stall in February but is not the force of old and was safely held in a C&D handicap last month.
runners.comment: Won handicap from wide stall in February but is not the force of old

runners.name: Stellar Express
runners.spotlight: 6yo who has shown remarkable improvement over 5f here this year, with one of his three wins gained in a Group 3 handicap; managed only sixth in C&D handicap last month, though, and faces tough task in this company.
runners.comment: In excellent form over 5f here this year but faces a tough task today

runners.name: Tomodachi Kokoroe
runners.spotlight: Career highlight when winning C&D Group 2 handicap in October; not far below that form when fourth in Class 1 C&D handicap last month but looks vulnerable on these terms.
runners.comment: Won Group 2 handicap here in October but looks vulnerable on this terms

runners.name: Lucky With You
runners.spotlight: Can hold his own in Group company when at his best but is yet to be placed after six runs this season, and finished last in a C&D handicap on his latest appearance.
runners.comment: Has held his own in Group company when at his best but is yet to be placed this season

races.name: Harlech Handicap (Class 3) (Course B+2) (Turf)

runners.name: Packing Glory
runners.spotlight: Won over 5f on debut here in November and placed on three of his four starts since, most recently when close third behind Fit For Beauty and Mighty Masts over C&D three weeks ago; still unexposed over 7f; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Placed on three of four starts since winning debut, the latest over C&D; shortlisted
runners.owner: Lee Wan Keung & Lee Wong Wai Kuen

runners.name: Harold Win
runners.spotlight: Largely consistent 5yo who was placed from double-digit stall over C&D two starts ago and also ran well when fifth here last month; on a workable mark now, even before Nihola Yuen's 10lb claim is considered, and has a big role to play from stall 1.
runners.comment: Consistent 5yo who has slipped to a workable mark and is well drawn; big player

runners.name: Mighty Masts
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on two of his four Hong Kong starts, more recently when coming from a long way off the pace to go very close behind Fit For Beauty over C&D three weeks ago; deserted by Zac Purton today but still considered.
runners.comment: Went close from a long way off the pace over C&D last month; one to consider
runners.owner: 24/25 Chris So Wai Yin Trainer Syndicate (2)

runners.name: Dragon On Snow
runners.spotlight: Won 1m AW maiden in Ireland in October 2024 but, following a long absence, was unable to land a telling blow when eighth in C&D race won by Fit For Beauty three weeks ago; needs that outing to have brought him on.
runners.comment: Unable to land a telling blow when eighth over C&D on recent Hong Kong debut
runners.owner: Eddie Hon Hoi Kwong Dr Kelvin Chong Man Yuk Et Al

runners.name: Ka Ying Superb
runners.spotlight: Close second over C&D on seasonal debut in September but well beaten all three starts since, the latest after a break; can't be supported with any confidence.
runners.comment: Went close over C&D on seasonal debut but well beaten all three starts since

runners.name: The Red Hare
runners.spotlight: Opened Hong Kong account over C&D in December and has remained in good form over 1m this year; nudged up to another career-high mark today but Zac Purton keeps the ride; ought to be right in the thick of things.
runners.comment: Opened HK account over C&D in December and has remained in good form over 1m this year
runners.owner: Lo Yu Sai Jasmine Lo Siu Yu Et Al

runners.name: Golden Champ
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner over 6f/7f in New Zealand but made low-key Hong Kong debut here (6f) a fortnight ago and might need longer to acclimatise.
runners.comment: Four-time winner in NZ (6f/7f) but made low-key HK debut here (6f) last month

runners.name: Fit For Beauty
runners.spotlight: Just held on to pip Mighty Masts over C&D three weeks ago and is now 3-8 in Hong Kong; latest 6lb rise asks quite a lot of him but further progress is possible.
runners.comment: Just held on to pip Mighty Masts over C&D three weeks ago and is now 3-8 in Hong Kong

runners.name: Dreaming Together
runners.spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo; well down the field on first two Hong Kong starts (7f/1m2f) and remains best watched.
runners.comment: Well down the field on first two Hong Kong starts (7f/1m2f) and remains best watched

runners.name: Lucy In The Sky
runners.spotlight: Went close off today's mark over C&D in November and was repeatedly denied a clear run when midfield on his last two 6f starts; can be dangerous if his luck changes.
runners.comment: Went close over C&D in November; denied clear run when midfield on last two 6f starts

races.name: Chairman's Trophy (Group 2) (Course B+2) (Turf)

runners.name: Fast Network
runners.spotlight: Made the frame behind Hong Kong's superstar sprinter Ka Ying Rising three consecutive over 6f (twice at Group 1 level) before comfortably winning Class 1 handicap over that trip last month; not sure to benefit from this return to 1m (although he does stay this far) but Zac Purton evidently favours him over Voyage Bubble.
runners.comment: Smart sprinter; not sure to benefit from this return to 1m but he's Zac Purton's pick

runners.name: My Wish
runners.spotlight: Began this season with two emphatic wins, the latter in C&D Group 2 handicap in October; his improvement seems to have levelled off since, though, and a minor role might be his best hope on these terms.
runners.comment: Began season in excellent form but his improvement seems to have levelled off
runners.owner: Ada Che Xiao Hong Suki Tang Xianfang & Ruby Hui Like Sea

runners.name: Lucky Sweynesse
runners.spotlight: Top-class 6f/7f performer in his prime (four Group 1 wins); no longer the force of old and inconsistency has crept in but he was placed at this level on both starts this year (1m/7f) and has each-way claims again.
runners.comment: Not quite the force of old but placed in Group 1s on both starts this season (6f/7f)
runners.owner: Cheng Ming Leung Cheng Yu Tung Et Al

runners.name: Galaxy Patch
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D Group 2 winner, latterly in November; not at best in C&D Group 1 in January but gave good account over 7f six weeks ago and, now back at his optimum trip, probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Dual C&D Group 2 winner; back up in trip after very respectable 7f run six weeks ago

runners.name: Sunlight Power
runners.spotlight: Excelled himself when close second to Galaxy Patch in C&D Group 2 in November but was soundly beaten (albeit over 7f) on latest outing and, on balance, others in this line-up look much stronger.
runners.comment: Excelled himself when second in Group 2 in November but, on balance, others are stronger

runners.name: Voyage Bubble
runners.spotlight: Six-time Group 1 winner, most recently in Hong Kong Mile over C&D in December; needs to rebound from below-par 1m2f run last month but will be very hard to beat if back on song.
runners.comment: Below form over 1m2f last time but this six-time Group 1 winner still commands respect

runners.name: Beauty Joy
runners.spotlight: Veteran who looked as good as ever when winning 1m1f Group 3 handicap here towards end of last season but has not been firing on all cylinders in recent months and faces a tough task today anyway.
runners.comment: Not firing on all cylinders in recent months and faces a tough task today anyway
runners.owner: Eleanor Kwok Law Kwai Chun & Patrick Kwok Ho Chuen

runners.name: Rubylot
runners.spotlight: Versatile 5yo who came second to Voyage Bubble in 1m4f Group 1 here at end of last season and stayed on for very respectable fifth behind My Wish in 7f Group 3 handicap on reappearance outing in September; three down-the-field finishes has followed, though, and he faces a tall order today.
runners.comment: Versatile 5yo; smart at best but finished down the field on last three appearances
runners.owner: HK Football Club Horse Racing Syndicate

runners.name: Storm Rider
runners.spotlight: Three-time handicap winner this season, including 7f Group 3 in January; yet to convince over 1m, though, and has a lot to find today.
runners.comment: Three-time handicap winner this season (6f-7f) but has a lot to find today

runners.name: Stunning Peach
runners.spotlight: Bang-in-form 5yo who recorded narrow wins in Class 2 handicaps here (1m/1m1f) on his last two outings; can't be recommended from stall 14 at this level, though.
runners.comment: Narrowly won Class 2 handicaps on last two outings; this will be much tougher

runners.name: Chancheng Glory
runners.spotlight: Not at best since finishing second in 1m1f dirt Group 3 in South Korea in September and looks up against it today.
runners.comment: Not at best since finishing second in 1m1f dirt Group 3 in South Korea in September

runners.name: Patch Of Theta
runners.spotlight: Won 7f Group 3 handicap at start of 2025 and was also successful in Class 1 handicap over that trip in February but 1m appears to stretch him and he faces a very tough assignment from stall 13.
runners.comment: 7f Group 3 handicap winner; 1m stretches him; this will be tough from stall 13

runners.name: Cap Ferrat
runners.spotlight: Narrow winner of last season's Hong Kong Derby (1m2f) and subsequently placed behind Voyage Bubble in 1m4f Group 1; persistently denied clear run when midfield on reappearance outing in October and absent since pulled up (went wrong) the following month; probably still has potential but this drop back to 1m is not ideal and he might be best watched on his comeback.
runners.comment: Last year's Derby hero; not much has gone to plan this season; returns from a break

runners.name: Red Lion
runners.spotlight: Caused 90-1 shock when clinging on narrowly in C&D Group 1 last April and put some disappointing efforts behind him when 51-1 third in Hong Kong Mile in December; well beaten on last two outings, though, and remains hard to predict.
runners.comment: Placed at big odds in Hong Kong Mile but well beaten twice since; very hard to predict
runners.owner: The Hon Ronald Arculli GBM GBS JP & Johanna K J Arculli BBS

races.name: Barker Handicap (Class 2) (Course B+2) (Turf)

runners.name: Lo Rider
runners.spotlight: Began season with 1m win at Happy Valley but his subsequent form has been underwhelming and he's seemingly still on a tough mark for this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Began season with 1m win at Happy Valley but his subsequent form has been underwhelming

runners.name: Steps Ahead
runners.spotlight: Very consistent over 7f/1m in first few months of this season but returned from three-month break with duff effort a fortnight ago and has the outside draw today.
runners.comment: Consistent in early part of season but returned from break with duff effort last month
runners.owner: Yeung Sing Tung Sunny Yeung Wah Hon & Janice Yeung Wah Kei

runners.name: Akashvani
runners.spotlight: Safely held in first two legs of this year's Classic Series (1m/1m1f) but was a very convincing C&D winner on his latest handicap start in January, and the booking of Zac Purton catches the eye.
runners.comment: Convincing C&D winner on latest handicap start; will find this easier than last two races

runners.name: Healthy Happy
runners.spotlight: Now only 1lb higher than when gamely fending off Steps Ahead over C&D in October but has not quite been in the same form this year and younger rivals have more appealing profiles.
runners.comment: C&D winner in October but not quite in same form lately; younger rivals preferred
runners.owner: Sin Tung Hing Sin Yuk Lok & Sin Yuk San

runners.name: Drombeg Banner
runners.spotlight: Close second twice over C&D this season but this steadily regressive 7yo has become inconsistent and remains without a win since 2024; others look safer.
runners.comment: Close second twice over C&D this season but this regressive 7yo has become inconsistent
runners.owner: Clement Ma Kang Ching Josiah Ma Pok Man & David Ma Yeuk Lai

runners.name: Mugen
runners.spotlight: Seemingly back in good form when third over C&D in March, his fourth run for Brett Crawford, but disappointed here a fortnight ago and Zac Purton has jumped ship.
runners.comment: Seemingly back in form two starts ago but well held since; deserted by Zac Purton

runners.name: Six Pack
runners.spotlight: Came good in Hong Kong with narrow C&D win on New Year's Day, his seventh attempt, and has made the frame over C&D on all three subsequent outings, most recently when close second to Winning Ovation a fortnight ago; likely contender again.
runners.comment: In the frame all three starts since C&D win in January; likely contender again

runners.name: Winning Ovation
runners.spotlight: Improving 4yo who returned from a layoff with two C&D wins last month and is now 3-4 in Hong Kong; could still have more to offer and commands respect.
runners.comment: Improving 4yo who returned from a layoff with two C&D wins last month and is now 3-4 in HK

runners.name: New Forest
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; in excellent form on dirt this season, winning three times, but struggled when back on turf for last two starts and remains on a daunting mark on this surface.
runners.comment: Three dirt wins this season; struggled when back on turf for last two runs

runners.name: Johannes Brahms
runners.spotlight: Placed in last season's Classic Cup (1m1f) before creditable sixth in Hong Kong Derby (1m2f) but very little has gone to plan this term and he needs to get his career back on track; eyeshields fitted for first time.
runners.comment: Very little has gone to plan this term and he needs to get his career back on track

races.name: Homestead Handicap (Class 3) (Course B+2) (Turf)

runners.name: Money Catcher
runners.spotlight: Not getting any younger but was placed on three of his four starts since exploiting much reduced mark at Happy Valley (1m1f) in January and has each-way claims again.
runners.comment: Placed on three of four starts since winning off much reduced mark in January

runners.name: Enthusium
runners.spotlight: Looked promising in Britain last year but made just a mildly encouraging Hong Kong debut here (1m) in February and finished well down the field behind Fallon at Happy Valley (1m1f) last month.
runners.comment: Made mildly encouraging Hong Kong debut in February but well down the field since

runners.name: The Auspicious
runners.spotlight: Three wins this season (7f/1m/1m3f); didn't really fire here (1m1f) a fortnight ago, though, and probably needs to find hidden reserves to overcome current mark.
runners.comment: Three wins this season; didn't fire last time, though, and remains on tough mark

runners.name: Mighty Strength
runners.spotlight: Caused 55-1 shock here (1m1f) on seasonal debut in October but not in same form on any of three starts since, albeit he didn't run badly when sixth at Happy Valley (1m1f) last month; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Shock winner here (1m1f) in October but not in same form on any of three starts since
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Li Ming On Joyce Li Lok Yan & Henry Li Lok Him

runners.name: Family Jewel
runners.spotlight: Safely held on last month's belated seasonal debut but fared better when fourth here (1m) eight days ago, and will be suited by this move back up in trip; high on the list.
runners.comment: Fourth over 1m recently, only his second start this season, and this trip suits better
runners.owner: Tony Stradmoor Billy Li Sze Kuen Et Al

runners.name: Fortune Boy
runners.spotlight: Now 12lb higher than when winning over 1m in first-time blinkers in October but went close over C&D two starts ago and again ran well when fourth here (1m1f) last time; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: On career-high mark but ran well here on both outings last month (1m2f/1m1f)
runners.owner: Ricky Wong Kin Chat & Helen Leung Yee Wan

runners.name: Smart Avenue
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive 4yo who went close here on consecutive outings last month (1m/1m1f); creeping up the weights but this new trip might unlock further improvement.
runners.comment: Went close here twice last month (1m/1m1f); upped in trip again today; respected
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Chan Chung Yin & Mr & Mrs Clevin Got Chong Key

runners.name: Aerodynamics
runners.spotlight: 3-4 when based in South Africa and has made the frame on two of his five Hong Kong starts, more recently when close fourth over C&D two starts ago; disappointed in first-time cheekpieces (retained) over 1m1f a fortnight ago but Zac Purton keeps the faith; big player if back on song.
runners.comment: Below par last time but Zac Purton keeps the faith; big player if back on song
runners.owner: Daniel Yeung Ngai & Gwenny Wu Pui Kwan

runners.name: Liveandletlive
runners.spotlight: Habitual front-runner; went very close on consecutive appearances (one over C&D) in January/February and quickly bounced back from a poor run when fourth here (1m1f) a fortnight ago; can give this a good shot.
runners.comment: Front-runner who has made the frame several times this season (once over C&D)

runners.name: Karma
runners.spotlight: Finished well to go well close here (1m1f) three starts ago but has not been at his best this season and is always reliant upon others setting a strong pace; others preferred.
runners.comment: Went close here in February but has not been at his best this season; others preferred
runners.owner: Manu N Melwani & Roshan Manohar Melwani

runners.name: Fallon
runners.spotlight: Kept on well to exploit reduced mark at Happy Valley (1m1f) last month, his fifth Hong Kong win; will be fine over the extra furlong here but recent 7lb rise is probably enough to leave him vulnerable.
runners.comment: Exploited reduced mark at Happy Valley last month; looks vulnerable after 7lb rise
